worldClassStrengths: The security spine as a fail-closed deterministic kernel the LLM cannot reason past. Three-zone classifier is an ALLOWLIST (GREEN = only `echo`/`pwd`, gateway.py:138-141) with RED-by-default and fail-closed in every direction — empty input, unknown command, ANY internal exception → RED (gateway.py:296-297, 348-350), proven by test_fail_closed_on_internal_exception. This is genuinely world-class because the safe-by-default posture is structural, not a blocklist that an attacker enumerates around.

The SHA-256 hash-chained audit ledger with cross-process integrity AND redact-before-hash ordering. A process threading.Lock + SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE extends the head-read+append critical section across worker processes; proven by a 4×20 concurrent-append test landing ONE valid 80-entry chain (test_concurrent_appends_keep_one_valid_chain). The non-obvious insight — redacting the payload BEFORE hashing — is what lets no-secret-persistence and chain-validity coexist; naive implementations hash the raw payload and break one or the other. Genesis = 64 zeros, O(n) verify with precise broken_at + linkage-vs-tamper distinction (audit_logger.py:115-270).

The 'RED un-grantable even after human approval' invariant — verified in source. Executor.execute_approved re-classifies and refuses RED even after one-click approval (executor.py:436-443: 'Human approval cannot authorise a RED action'). This is stricter than the blueprint's own typed-token override spec, and it makes human authority a widening-only surface, never a bypass of the hard floor. This is the load-bearing line of the whole trust model and it is real code under test (test_execute_approved_still_refuses_red).

The self-application path with structural (not just procedural) no-self-approval. SelfApplyEngine: snapshot → git apply --check → audit-before-write (fail-closed: no ledger entry → no write) → single-file-confined apply → an INDEPENDENT two-snapshot byte-comparison integrity check → gated verify → auto-rollback. There is deliberately NO agent `apply` tool — applying is reachable only from a human HTTP endpoint, with approver≠proposer as a labeled second layer (self_apply.py:13-18). Getting the structural-first / comparison-second ordering right (you don't rely on a check you could also delete) is supply-chain-grade caution most prototypes skip entirely.

The stigmergic learning layer — the most mature subsystem in the repo. Saturating, deliberately ASYMMETRIC pheromone (one failure ≈ seven successes, exact constants 0.70805 / 1.04252 pinned by test), with a hard correctness boundary: DIRECT verifier evidence is the only thing that can promote to 'verified'; REUSE/co-occurrence pheromone can rank but can NEVER launder a candidate into trusted. Promotion gates ≥3 verified successes AND ≥0.8 rate; a later verified failure demotes. db._migrate SUPERSEDES trail rows rather than DELETEing because they are irreplaceable verifier evidence (memory/skills.py).

The earned-autonomy bridge — verified in source as evidence-gated, off-by-default, RED-un-earnable, one-failure-revokes. autonomy.py docstring + AutonomyLedger: a YELLOW action class graduates to autonomous execution ONLY on accumulated VERIFIED-success evidence (min 5), keyed by the SHAPE of the action (type + scope-bound target pattern, values stripped + secret-redacted) so it earns as a bounded class within the scope the gateway already enforces; one verified failure resets the streak instantly; every auto-grant is a distinct audit-chain entry under actor 'earned-autonomy' with the earning streak as evidence. This is the same pheromone logic as the skill trails, applied to autonomy — a coherent, non-trivial generalization.

The test discipline itself: 90% live-measured line coverage of aios/ (5087 stmts, 510 missed, lowest module 81%), 512 passing tests across three suites, with the dominant 'real gateway, fake runner' pattern — fakes are ONLY the non-deterministic edges (LLM chat, shell spawn, embedder); the security/scope/audit/verify machinery runs for real, so tests prove GENUINE refusal, not mocked returns. Negative-path-dominant (self_apply: 18 tests, ~15 refusal/rollback). Distinctive contracts under test: a VERIFICATION RED event provably never plays the success tick (soundEngine.test.ts); the adapter's honest LINK OFFLINE degradation is itself tested.

The superbrain frontend bound to real backend data with enforced honest dormancy. One ~67-line module-singleton pub/sub (cognitionBus.ts) is what makes six subsystems read as one organism; the approval 'hold' is a single product thesis rendered across five files (breath freezes mid-inhale, cortex/wires/aura tint amber, starfield clock dilates to 30%, camera dollies, suspended-2nd chord). Crucially: no trails → no stars, link down → LINK OFFLINE not faked activity — the same no-decorative-lies discipline as the backend, and tested as such.
uniqueDifferentiators: Evidence-over-model enforced as control flow, not as a guideline, carried without compromise across SIX independently-built subsystems. Everywhere a lesser design lets the LLM's word stand ('this is safe' / 'I'm confident' / 'the tests passed' / 'this was approved'), a piece of non-LLM code re-derives or re-proves the claim: gateway re-classifies, verifier reads exit codes as authoritative, confidence is calibrated only by VERIFIED memory, approval re-classifies RED. The CONSISTENCY of this discipline is the rare thing — most systems apply it in one place and trust the model everywhere else.

Earned, revocable, RED-un-earnable autonomy driven by the SAME pheromone math as skill learning. Autonomy is not a config flag or a trust score — it is graduated by accumulated verifier evidence, keyed to a bounded action SHAPE inside gateway-enforced scope, instantly revoked by a single verified failure, and recorded as its own audit-chain entry. A supervised system that can mechanically, auditably WIDEN its own autonomous surface from proven evidence — while keeping a hard floor human authority cannot lower — is a genuinely distinctive control architecture.

The self-improving loop closes the full circle with structural anti-self-approval. The OS analyzes its own source (self-analysis T0–T4), proposes diffs, and applies them — but the only path to apply is a human HTTP endpoint with byte-comparison integrity + auto-rollback + frozen-core RED refusal, and the agent has no apply tool at all. Self-modification that is structurally unable to approve itself is rare even in well-funded agent systems.

The living-mind interface is a faithful read-out of the supervised cage, not a dashboard bolted on top. The approval pause — the literal product thesis (a mind deferring to its operator) — is rendered simultaneously across breath, color, time-dilation, camera, and sound, driven by the SAME SSE frames the backend emits. The UI's honesty (dormant when there's no data) is the backend's honesty extended to pixels. The marriage of an evidence-locked core to a living-mind face, where the face cannot lie because it's wired to real frames, is the strongest first-of-its-kind seed.

Single-operator, local-first, two-week supervised build that nonetheless ships a coordination control plane with content-addressed integrity. agent_coord.py binds a review verdict cryptographically to the exact bytes reviewed (tree_snapshot hashes HEAD + tracked diff + sorted untracked bytes; record_verdict fail-closes on ANY post-handoff drift and forbids self-approval). Most solo projects never build cross-agent integrity at all, let alone hash-pinned.
calibre.frontierReadiness: "Honest read: this is a genuine, coherent, internally-consistent supervised AI-OS whose deterministic foundation is at frontier-grade ENGINEERING quality and proven at the level of its contracts — but it is not yet a frontier AI SYSTEM, and the gates are intelligence and isolation, not architecture. The mechanism layer is built to receive a better brain; it does not yet have one. Concretely, three things gate it: (1) the 7–8B local model ceiling — planning is advisory, calibration is a no-op on a cold DB, castes are explicitly 'architecture proven / 7B-limited,' and curriculum matching is exact-prompt-string, so organic intelligence won't materially improve until a 14B+ local model and a semantic-recall layer land; (2) the autonomous surface is near-zero BY DESIGN (host-mode GREEN allowlist is two patterns) — correct for a supervised OS, but it means the system is a supervised assistant that can safely act, not yet a system that genuinely runs itself, until earned-autonomy is opened and the evidence→GREEN bridge is widened; (3) strong OS isolation is OPT-IN — the hardened DockerRunner is real but the default host backend is candidly not an isolation boundary, so the strongest trust guarantee depends on Docker being present. None of these are defects in what exists; they are the deliberate, documented edges of what exists. The unglamorous hard part — the evidence-locked, fail-closed, audited, self-modifying core — is the part most people never finish, and it is already built. The frontier is three moves away (better brain, default-strong isolation, widened earned autonomy), and the system is architected to receive all three."
honestCeilings: The local model is the intelligence ceiling. 7–8B Ollama: planning is advisory only, _calibrate is a no-op on a cold DB (no verified memory → raw model self-report runs the gate), castes are 'architecture proven / 7B-limited,' curriculum matching is exact-prompt-string (live-proven via a controlled run, narrow for organic chat). The mechanism is built for a 14B+ brain it doesn't have; intelligence won't materially improve until the RAM/model situation does.

Two named lexical recall seams. Skill / lesson / curriculum recall is purely LEXICAL despite a full semantic FAISS stack sitting right there for semantic_memory — a workflow phrased in different vocabulary than its goal may not be recalled; and BM25 runs ONLY over the FAISS candidate pool, so a pure-lexical match FAISS never surfaces is missed. Both are documented tradeoffs and the obvious next retrieval lever.

No forgetting / no eviction. Episodic is append-only with no pruning; semantic is only ever superseded, never aged out; working memory has no TTL despite its docstring; _index_turn writes unverified chat every answered turn with only recall-tiering as counter-pressure. On a long-running install these grow monotonically and unverified chat can come to dominate the candidate pool. There is promotion but no eviction — the missing half of consolidation.

The autonomous surface is near-zero by design. Host-mode GREEN auto-execute is two patterns (echo, pwd); everything real — pytest, edits, installs, git — is YELLOW and needs a human. Earned-autonomy is the one bridge and it is OFF by default. Correct for a supervised OS, but it means unattended capability is essentially nil unless explicitly opened.

Strong OS isolation is opt-in, not default. The hardened DockerRunner (--network none --read-only --cap-drop ALL --user 65534) is real and tested, but the default host backend runs approved arbitrary code as the backend OS user and is candidly documented as 'not an OS isolation boundary.' The strongest guarantee depends on Docker being present; on this Windows laptop that's a real operational dependency.

The audit chain has no external anchoring. A local SHA-256 hash chain without an out-of-band root of trust: an attacker with full write access who recomputes the entire chain forward from a tampered entry produces a self-consistent forgery. Inherent to the single-laptop threat model, not an implementation defect — but periodic off-box notarization of the head hash is the fix the moment this matters enough to attack.

Voice = 0%, full knowledge graph = not built, observability stack = not built. Blueprint-specified Whisper/Piper STT/TTS has no code or dependency anywhere; the 'knowledge graph' is a flat SQLite triple store with contradiction detection (no traversal beyond facts_for(subject), the only graph-shaped code is dead legacy_node/); Prometheus/Grafana/compose are documentation-only. All three are honestly deferred, not broken — but they are absent.

Deploy/observability gaps in the default face. The superbrain aiosAdapter sends NO Authorization header (only the classic config.js wires a Bearer token), so a token-protected/non-loopback deploy of the DEFAULT UI gets 401 on every call; there is no app-level uvicorn entrypoint (AIOS_API_HOST/PORT are read by the policy check but never bind the socket); and a live AWS Bedrock bearer token sits in plaintext in frontend/.env (gitignored, verified never in git history, but a real credential in the wrong place — rotate). The 3D scene/HUD/ApprovalPanel/shaders have ZERO unit tests — correctness rests on 37 golden PNGs + 15 puppeteer probes a human eyeballs, so a regression in wave anchoring or hold choreography would not fail CI.
calibre.firstOfItsKindThesis: "The category this creates is the evidence-locked supervised AI operating system: a weak local model proposes, but deterministic, tested, hash-chain-audited machinery decides — and that single invariant ('trust the evidence, not the model') is carried without compromise through security, cognition, memory, learning, self-modification, autonomy, AND the interface. What is genuinely novel is the fusion of three things that almost never appear together: a fail-closed deterministic cage the LLM cannot reason past, an earned/revocable/RED-un-earnable autonomy bridge graduated by the same verifier evidence (not by model confidence) that the system also uses to learn skills, and a living-mind 3D interface that is a faithful real-data read-out of that supervised cage — a face that cannot lie because it is wired to the same SSE frames the backend emits and goes honestly dormant when there is no data. The seed is real, not aspirational: the RED-un-grantable line, the redact-before-hash audit chain, the structural no-self-approval, and the evidence-keyed autonomy ledger are all verified in source and under behavior-level test."

advices.persona: Frontier cognitive-architecture researcher (agentic reasoning, neuro-symbolic systems, world models, test-time compute) advising the ai-editor evidence-locked supervised AI-OS
advices.horizonThesis: The defining move available here is unique in the field: this system already has a *verifier that is the judge* (exit-code-authoritative `Verifier`, `_auto_verify`, RED-un-grantable gateway) and a *pheromone evidence economy* (skill trails, earned-autonomy ledger) — which means it can host real System-2 deliberation WITHOUT the usual danger, because every branch the model explores is scored by deterministic evidence rather than by the model's own self-grade. Today the loop is pure System-1: `ToolAgent.run` (tool_agent.py:667) goes model.chat → _dispatch with no search, no candidate comparison, no world-model; the `Planner` exists but is advisory-only, never in the act loop (main.py:806 vs 1495). The frontier is to fold deliberate search, self-consistency, and a learned world-model INTO the loop as *proposers feeding the same verifier-judge* — turning "trust the evidence not the model" from a safety floor into an active reasoning engine. The cognitive architecture upgrade is not a smarter model; it is more *deliberation per unit of evidence*, all of which the existing cage already adjudicates.

recommendations.title: Verifier-judged tree search: promote the advisory Planner into a real System-2 deliberation layer that proposes branches the cage scores
recommendations.what: Insert a deliberate planning/search step BETWEEN model.chat and _dispatch in ToolAgent.run (tool_agent.py:667-711). Instead of executing the model's first tool call, sample K candidate next-actions (K=2-3, cheap on a 7B), have the existing Planner decompose+confidence-gate each, and pick the branch by a deterministic score = confidence_filter result + verified-skill-trail match (skills.relevant_verified) + a cheap dry-run check (git apply --check for edits, gateway.classify zone, _expected_after byte-diff for self-apply). Crucially: NOTHING executes until the chosen branch passes the SAME gateway→approval→verifier path it does today. The verifier stays the sole judge of OUTCOME; the search only re-orders PROPOSALS. Surface it as a new optional ToolAgent mode (AIOS_DELIBERATE) and a `deliberation` SSE frame so the superbrain can render branch-consideration as a real read-out.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Test-time tree search (ToT/LATS-style) is normally gated by an LLM self-evaluator that can hallucinate its own goodness. Here the branch selector is grounded in DETERMINISTIC, already-built signals (gateway zone, git-apply-check, verified pheromone strength) — a search whose value function is non-LLM evidence. No agent system I know of runs deliberate search where the candidate-scorer is a fail-closed deterministic kernel rather than the model judging itself.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The Planner is fully built with verified-evidence calibration (planner.py:147 _calibrate, bounded lesson/history/skill adjustments) but is advisory-only — wired to /api/v1/plan (main.py:806) and the `plan` tool, never into the act loop (the loop at tool_agent.py:667 dispatches the model's first call directly). The confidence_filter, SkillMemory.relevant_verified, and Verifier already exist as the scoring primitives. This is wiring an existing engine into the loop, not inventing one.
recommendations.risk: K-sampling multiplies local-model latency on a GPU already shared with the LLM (a real perf hotspot per HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE); a weak 7B may produce K near-identical branches so search adds cost without diversity until a 14B+ brain lands. Mitigation: cap K=2, gate behind a flag, and make the deterministic dry-run (git apply --check) the dominant tiebreaker so even degenerate branches are ranked by real evidence. Must NOT let search widen the autonomous surface — selection only re-orders proposals; YELLOW still pauses.

recommendations.title: Self-consistency over the verifier, not over the model: N-sample edit/fix generation adjudicated by sibling-pytest
recommendations.what: For propose_fixes (Self-Analysis T2) and edit_file/create_file in training_ground, generate N candidate diffs (N=3) instead of one, apply each to an isolated temp copy (reusing SelfApplyEngine's existing two-snapshot _expected_after machinery), run the file's sibling pytest via the gated Verifier on EACH candidate, and keep ONLY the candidate(s) that pass — choosing among passers by minimal-diff/lowest-complexity. If zero pass, report UNVERIFIED honestly (today's behavior) rather than picking the model's favorite. This is self-consistency where the consistency check is the AUTHORITATIVE exit code, not a majority vote of model opinions.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Classic self-consistency (Wang et al.) marginalizes over reasoning paths by majority vote of the MODEL's own answers. Here the 'vote' is replaced by an authoritative test-execution verdict per candidate — making it self-consistency-by-verification. Combined with the structural no-self-approval (no agent apply tool, self_apply.py:13-18), you get the first self-modifying loop that generates a population of fixes and lets a deterministic test harness, not the LLM, elect the survivor.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: _auto_verify already force-runs sibling pytest after a write (tool_agent.py:881, force-verify-after-write) and treats exit code as authoritative; SelfApplyEngine already does independent two-snapshot byte-comparison + gated verify + auto-rollback (self_apply.py); T2 propose_open already drafts unified-diff fixes via an injected completion LLM without applying. The N→1 election is a population wrapper around machinery that already verifies and rolls back one candidate.
recommendations.risk: N× verifier runs cost N× pytest invocations — bounded but real on a laptop; mitigate by N=3 and early-exit on first passer for non-self-analysis edits. A subtler risk: if all N pass spuriously (weak sibling test), the election is only as good as the test — but that is the SAME ceiling the system already accepts, and reporting UNVERIFIED on zero-pass preserves honesty. Must keep self-apply human-endpoint-only; this changes candidate generation, never the apply authority.

aios/core/verifier.py
aios/agents/tool_agent.py (_auto_verify)

recommendations.title: A learned, evidence-grounded world-model of the SANDBOX as a predictive cache — not a fantasy simulator
recommendations.what: Build a lightweight deterministic 'sandbox world-model': a learned predictor over the action→outcome pairs the system ALREADY records (gateway zone, verify PASS/FAIL, files touched, skill-trail arc) that, before executing a YELLOW/GREEN action, predicts the likely verifier verdict and likely scope/zone, and surfaces that prediction in the deliberation frame. Critically the world-model is EVIDENCE-DERIVED (built only from verified outcomes in the audit ledger + skill trails) and is ADVISORY: it can re-rank branches and warn 'this edit class has a 0.2 verified-pass rate' but it can NEVER substitute for the real verifier run. The verifier still executes and remains the judge; the world-model just makes search cheaper by pruning predicted-failures before paying for pytest.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: World-models in agentic systems usually try to SIMULATE outcomes to AVOID acting (and hallucinate). This world-model is deliberately epistemically humble: it's a predictive prior trained on the system's own tamper-evident verified history, used only to order/prune candidate branches, with the ground-truth verifier always run before anything is trusted. A world-model that is structurally forbidden from replacing the evidence it was trained to predict is a genuinely novel safety posture.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: DevelopmentTracker.relevant_success_rate (development.py:73) already computes relevance-weighted verified success rates; the audit ledger is a complete action→zone history; SkillMemory trail_map (skills.py:301) already exposes per-arc verified pass-rate/freshness/strength. The world-model is a thin predictive read-model over these existing verified stores — the pheromone economy IS a primitive world-model already; this names and operationalizes it.
recommendations.risk: A predictor trained on a cold/small DB (the documented calibration no-op problem) predicts garbage — so it must fail-OPEN to 'no prediction, run the verifier' on low evidence, exactly like _calibrate is a no-op on a cold DB today. Real danger if anyone lets the prediction GATE execution (skipping the verifier to 'save time') — that would violate evidence-over-model. Hard invariant: prediction may prune the search frontier but the chosen action ALWAYS gets a real verifier verdict before it is trusted or earns autonomy.

recommendations.title: Semantic episodic recall as the missing System-2 substrate: unify the lexical recall seams onto the existing FAISS stack with a verified-tier guard
recommendations.what: Route skill/lesson/curriculum recall (today purely lexical, relevance.py token-set cosine) through the SAME FAISS semantic stack that already powers semantic_memory — so a deliberation step can retrieve relevant PAST verified workflows by meaning, not vocabulary, and feed them as candidate branches to the tree search (rec #1). Keep BM25 as a re-ranker but run it over a UNION of FAISS candidates AND a global lexical pass (closing the 'BM25 only over FAISS pool' seam). Preserve the verified/unverified tiering exactly as today (_recall_memory's VERIFIED TRUSTED vs UNVERIFIED LEAD) so semantic recall never launders unverified chat into evidence.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Most agent memories retrieve by similarity and trust what they retrieve. Here, semantic retrieval feeds a deliberation engine whose retrieved items are still tier-labeled by VERIFICATION status — the recall is semantic but the TRUST is evidentiary. A reasoning loop that can find 'I solved something like this before' by meaning, yet refuses to treat an unverified semantic match as proof, is the right substrate for grounded System-2 over long-running memory.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The full semantic FAISS stack (embeddings.py IndexIDMap, crash-consistent writes, cross-process reload) is BUILT and live for semantic_memory but explicitly NOT used for skill/lesson/curriculum recall — both seams are documented in BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md §Gaps as 'the obvious next retrieval lever.' This connects two things already in the repo.
recommendations.risk: Cold-start MiniLM load on the request thread is a known latency hotspot (embeddings.py:42); semantic recall on every turn adds cost — mitigate with the existing empty-index short-circuit and lazy singleton. Subtler: semantic recall could surface MORE unverified chat into the candidate pool (the no-forgetting problem), so it MUST ship alongside rec-adjacent eviction or it amplifies the monotonic-growth ceiling. Keep the verified-tier prompt guard absolutely intact.

recommendations.title: Consolidation/forgetting as a 'sleep' pass — the missing half of the cognitive loop, gated by the verifier and audit-anchored
recommendations.what: Add a deliberate, scheduled-or-on-demand consolidation pass (the 'sleep' the docstrings promise but never implement) that does eviction, not just promotion: age out / compact unverified episodic chat under a TTL, decay-evict stale unused semantic rows (superseded already exists), and — the System-2 part — run a REFLECTION pass over recent verified failures to synthesize higher-order lessons/skills, each of which must itself pass the existing evidence gates before becoming trusted. Record every consolidation decision as a distinct audit-chain entry (actor 'consolidation') so forgetting is itself tamper-evident and auditable.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Agentic memories almost never forget, and when they consolidate they do it by LLM summarization that can rewrite history. Here consolidation is evidence-gated (only verified lessons promote, MemoryConsolidator already enforces this) AND audit-anchored (forgetting leaves a hash-chain trace), so the system can compact its mind without ever silently losing or fabricating the verifier evidence that is its irreplaceable trust substrate — note db._migrate already SUPERSEDES rather than DELETEs trail rows for exactly this reason.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: MemoryConsolidator.run/consolidate_lesson/promote_fact already exists and is evidence-gated; the 'no forgetting / no eviction' gap is named explicitly in BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md and HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE.md ('you have promotion; you don't yet have eviction'); WorkingMemory has a TTL docstring but no TTL; db._migrate already demonstrates the supersede-not-delete discipline for irreplaceable evidence.
recommendations.risk: Eviction is the one place you could destroy verifier evidence — the cardinal sin. Hard invariant: NEVER delete a row carrying direct verified success/failure counts (skill trails, verified lessons); only compact UNVERIFIED chat and SUPERSEDE stale semantic rows, exactly as the existing migration does. A buggy TTL that evicts a verified trail would silently lower the system's intelligence with no error — so every consolidation decision must be audit-logged and reversible via the rollback discipline, and dry-run-able before it acts.

advices.northStar: Wire the existing advisory Planner into the act loop as a verifier-judged tree search (rec #1) and pair it with self-consistency-by-verification (rec #2): a deliberate System-2 layer that proposes a SMALL population of branches/fixes per step and lets the already-built deterministic cage — gateway zone, git-apply-check, sibling-pytest exit codes — elect the survivor. This is the single move that turns 'trust the evidence, not the model' from a defensive floor into an active reasoning engine, and it is buildable on today's primitives with a 7B brain, getting dramatically better the moment a 14B+ model lands.
advices.honestCaveat: Be honest that none of this raises raw intelligence — it raises DELIBERATION per unit of evidence, and deliberation on a 7B model that emits near-identical branches will, for now, often cost latency without changing the answer (the GPU is already shared with the LLM). The verifier-judged search is genuinely novel as ARCHITECTURE, but its payoff is model-gated exactly like castes and calibration are today; the calibre brief is right that the gates are intelligence and isolation, not architecture, and a tree search over a model that can only think of one branch is theater. The world-model (rec #3) is the easiest to over-claim: it is a predictive read-model over verified history, NOT a simulator, and the instant anyone uses its prediction to SKIP a verifier run to save time, the whole evidence-over-model invariant is violated — so its honest framing is 'a cache that prunes search,' never 'a model of the world.' And consolidation/forgetting (rec #5) is the one enhancement that can actively make the system DUMBER if a TTL bug evicts verified evidence, so it is lower-priority than it looks and must be audit-anchored and reversible before it touches anything. Ship rec #2 and #4 first (near-term, low-risk, real wins); treat #1/#3/#5 as the frontier that genuinely arrives only with the better brain the mechanism is built to receive.

advices.persona: Recursive-autonomy / self-improving-systems scientist — the specialist on how a supervised system safely widens its own GREEN surface and learns from its own VERIFIED evidence without the model ever getting to vouch for itself.
advices.horizonThesis: This system has already crossed the line most "self-improving AI" projects never reach: the loop that closes (self-analysis → propose → human-gated apply → verify → rollback) and the bridge that graduates trust (earned-autonomy keyed to verifier evidence, RED-un-earnable, one-failure-revokes) are real code under test, not slideware. The frontier is NOT "let it edit itself more freely" — that violates the invariants. The frontier is making the EVIDENCE richer, the AUTONOMY graduation finer-grained and falsifiable, and the SELF-CURRICULUM grow off the system's OWN audited successes — so a weak local brain compounds verified competence while the human + RED-block stay exactly where they are. The single rarest move available: turn the audit chain from a passive ledger into the TRAINING SET for a self-distillation flywheel, so the system gets measurably better at the narrow things it has already PROVEN it can do, with every gain falsifiable on held-out evidence.

recommendations.title: Self-curriculum mined from the audit chain (the evidence flywheel)
recommendations.what: Add an offline, operator-triggered job that walks the SHA-256 audit ledger (aios_audit.db) + the verified skill trails (memory/skills.py) and synthesizes NEW curriculum tasks from sequences the system has ALREADY verified-passed at least N times: take a verified trail's goal-tokens + argument-stripped tool sequence (skill_signature_v2) and emit a held-out curriculum task (curriculum.py) whose 'training' examples are the audited passing runs and whose 'held-out' check is a fresh verifier run. The curriculum already gates mastery on '[VERIFY PASS]/[VERIFY FAIL]' evidence and a held-out pass; this just AUTO-POPULATES the tasks from the system's own proven history instead of requiring a human to hand-author each prompt. Nothing becomes 'mastered' without a fresh held-out verifier pass — the existing gate is the safety.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: A self-curriculum whose every task is provenance-traced to a tamper-evident audit entry and whose mastery is falsifiable on held-out verifier evidence is genuinely novel — it is curriculum learning where the LABEL is a cryptographically-anchored exit code, not a model's self-assessment. Most 'self-improving' agents bootstrap curricula from model-judged success, which launders confabulation into the training signal. This one structurally cannot.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: curriculum.py already refuses anything not prefixed [VERIFY PASS]/[VERIFY FAIL] and masters only after training_passes + full coverage + a held-out pass (BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md 'Held-out-gated curriculum mastery'); skills.py skill_signature_v2 + the audit chain (audit_logger.py:115-270) are the irreplaceable verifier-evidence source; record_outcome (main.py:1577) already writes curriculum evidence keyed strictly off the verdict.
recommendations.risk: The current curriculum trigger is EXACT-prompt-string (curriculum.py 'WHERE prompt = ?'), so auto-mined tasks may never fire on organic chat — this is honest scaffolding until a semantic-match layer lands (S1). Risk of curriculum bloat/monotonic growth (the same no-eviction debt as memory, HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE 'Growth stores have no forgetting'); mitigate by capping mined tasks and aging unmastered ones. It RAISES the value of a better brain but does not require one.
dependencies: Semantic-recall layer for curriculum/skill matching (PLAN.md S1) to make mined tasks actually trigger
Memory forgetting/compaction (PLAN.md S4) so mined curriculum doesn't grow unbounded

recommendations.title: Graduated probation tiers for earned autonomy (shadow-grant before live-grant)
recommendations.what: Insert an intermediate ledger state between 'probation' and 'earned' in autonomy.py: 'shadow'. A signature that reaches the success streak first becomes SHADOW-earned — on the next matching YELLOW action the system STILL pauses for the human, but it pre-computes and records (in the audit chain) the exact action it WOULD have auto-applied, and after the human's verdict lands it scores its own shadow-prediction against reality. Only signatures whose shadow predictions match the human's approved action M times in a row promote to live 'earned'. This adds a falsifiable dry-run gate so autonomy is granted on PROVEN agreement-with-human, not just streak length.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: This is counterfactual self-evaluation as the gate to autonomy: the system earns the right to act unattended by first PROVING, on the audited record, that its unattended choice would have matched the human's — a measurable, revocable 'do I agree with my operator?' score. No supervised agent I know graduates autonomy on shadow-mode agreement evidence rather than raw success counts; it makes the widening of the GREEN surface itself evidence-gated end-to-end.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: autonomy.py already has the exact state machine (probation→earned→revoked, streak reset on one failure, signature = secret-redacted action SHAPE) and every grant is a distinct audit entry under actor 'earned-autonomy' (autonomy.py:1-25, record_outcome path main.py:1503-1623); the human_required pause already carries the exact pending action (main.py:1624-1701) — shadow-scoring reuses that real diff/command.
recommendations.risk: Adds a state and more pauses before autonomy is granted — by design slower, which is correct for a supervised OS. Must NOT widen RED (execute_approved re-classification stays the floor, executor.py:436-443). Risk of complexity creep in the ledger; keep shadow-scoring purely additive and off by default behind the existing AIOS_EARNED_AUTONOMY flag so a deployment that never opts in is byte-identical to today.
dependencies: No new deps; builds entirely on autonomy.py + the existing human_required replay path

recommendations.title: Verifier diversity / N-of-M evidence quorum before any autonomy grant
recommendations.what: Today a single sibling-pytest PASS is the authoritative evidence for both skill promotion and the autonomy streak. Add a pluggable second independent verifier lane (e.g. a property/mutation check, a re-run in the hardened DockerRunner, or a static re-analysis of the written file via the existing T0/T1 AST pass) and require that a streak success counts ONLY when ≥2 independent verifier lanes agree. Make the quorum policy a config knob; default 1 (today's behavior) so nothing changes unless opted in. This hardens the evidence that earns autonomy against a single flaky/spoofable test.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: It generalizes 'trust the evidence, not the model' to 'trust EVIDENCE THAT AGREES WITH ITSELF, not a single oracle' — an N-of-M cryptographically-logged verifier quorum gating self-granted autonomy. The exit-code-authoritative verifier is already the system's spine; making autonomy require corroborated, independently-derived evidence is the natural frontier extension of the calibre's defining invariant.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: Verifier.verify is exit-code-authoritative and _auto_verify already force-runs sibling pytest after every write (BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md cognition core); the hardened DockerRunner (--network none --read-only --cap-drop ALL) and the deterministic T0/T1 AST analysis already exist as candidate second lanes; the fail-direction asymmetry pattern (gateway fail-closed) shows the team already reasons in these terms.
recommendations.risk: A second lane (esp. Docker re-run) adds latency on an already GPU/RAM-constrained 16GB laptop sharing bandwidth with the local LLM, and Docker is opt-in (HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE 'host execution mode is the default'). Mitigate by making the cheapest second lane (AST re-analysis) the default and Docker the opt-in. Must stay fail-closed: a lane that errors counts as DISAGREEMENT, never as silent pass.
dependencies: Default-strong isolation (PLAN.md S2) makes the Docker lane practical
Better local brain (S1) raises the ceiling on what mutation/property checks can catch

recommendations.title: Local self-distillation of the proposer off VERIFIED-only traces (the bounded recursive lever)
recommendations.what: Build an offline, operator-launched distillation job that exports a fine-tune/LoRA dataset consisting ONLY of audit-anchored verified-success traces (prompt → the exact tool sequence that produced a [VERIFY PASS], secrets already redacted by scan_and_redact), trains a small local adapter for the 7-8B Ollama proposer on that corpus, and gates the resulting adapter behind a held-out curriculum bake-off: the new adapter only becomes the active proposer if it masters the auto-mined self-curriculum at ≥ the incumbent's rate on FRESH held-out verifier runs. The model that proposes gets better at the narrow things the system has PROVEN, but it never self-certifies — a deterministic held-out gate promotes it, and the operator + RED-block are untouched.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: This is a closed self-distillation flywheel where the training data is a tamper-evident, secret-redacted, verifier-labeled ledger and the promotion gate is a held-out exit-code bake-off — a recursively self-improving PROPOSER that is structurally unable to launder its own confidence into the weights, because every label is an authoritative verdict and every promotion is falsifiable. That combination (audited-evidence training set + held-out verifier promotion gate + RED-un-earnable + human-revocable) is the genuinely first-of-its-kind recursive-self-improvement primitive this system is uniquely positioned to ship.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The audit chain is redact-before-hash so traces are already secret-free and tamper-evident (audit_logger.py:158-160); record_outcome writes verified_success/failure keyed strictly off the verifier (main.py:1716-1720); the curriculum held-out gate (curriculum.py) is the ready-made promotion oracle; the Ollama provider + model_selector already swap models deterministically (SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE.md §3.1). The 'mechanism built to receive a better brain' is exactly this socket.
recommendations.risk: Highest-ambition, most-honest-about-limits item: a 7-8B base has a hard ceiling (the whole calibre flags the model as THE intelligence gate), so gains will be narrow and may plateau; reward-hacking/overfitting to the verifier is the classic failure (mitigated by held-out-only promotion + verifier diversity from rec #3). Local fine-tune on 16GB is RAM-tight (favors a small LoRA, not full FT). This NEVER touches the security spine or RED, and the proposer is advisory — the cage still decides — so even a degenerate adapter cannot escalate; worst case it proposes worse diffs that the gate rejects.
dependencies: A better/larger local brain or at least LoRA-capable tooling (PLAN.md S1, RAM)
Self-curriculum mining (rec #1) to supply the held-out bake-off
Verifier quorum (rec #3) to harden the labels against reward-hacking

recommendations.title: Replayable self-improvement provenance: every grant/promotion reproducible from the ledger
recommendations.what: Add a deterministic 'replay' command that, given any earned-autonomy grant, skill promotion, or (future) adapter promotion, reconstructs the exact evidence chain from the audit ledger + trail rows and re-derives whether the grant is still justified — and a periodic off-box notarization of the audit head hash so the evidence base for self-improvement has an external root of trust. This makes the entire self-improving loop AUDITABLE AND REVOCABLE after the fact: an operator (or a future reviewer) can ask 'why does this class run unattended?' and get a reproducible, externally-anchored answer, and can revoke any link whose evidence no longer replays clean.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: A recursive-self-improvement system where every capability the machine granted ITSELF is replayable from a tamper-evident, externally-notarized ledger and revocable on replay-failure is a new accountability standard for self-modifying AI — provenance-complete autonomy. The audit chain already explains WHY a write ran without a human; this closes the loop by making that explanation independently reproducible and anchored beyond the laptop.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: Every auto-grant is already a distinct audit-chain entry carrying the earning streak as evidence (autonomy.py docstring; record_for puts the evidence into the entry); verify_chain already gives O(n) tamper detection with precise broken_at (audit_logger.py:115-270); the operator revoke path exists (autonomy.revoke, main.py:725-732); the calibre explicitly names 'no external anchoring' as the one inherent audit gap (HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE security surface).
recommendations.risk: Off-box notarization reintroduces a network/external dependency into an intentionally local-first, privacy-by-default system — must be operator-opt-in and notarize ONLY the head HASH (never payload), so privacy is preserved. Replay is read-only and cannot widen anything, so the risk is low; the main cost is keeping replay logic in lock-step with any future change to the grant criteria (version the criteria in the ledger entry).
dependencies: None hard; complements earned-autonomy and the self-distillation gate by making both reproducible

advices.northStar: Ship the audit-anchored self-distillation flywheel (rec #4) gated by the self-mined held-out curriculum (rec #1) and corroborated verifier evidence (rec #3): a weak local proposer that measurably improves at the narrow tasks it has PROVEN, where every training label is a tamper-evident exit code, every promotion is falsifiable on fresh held-out evidence, and the human + RED-block sit exactly where they are today. That is bounded, verifiable, revocable recursive self-improvement — the prime version of what this system already seeds.
advices.honestCaveat: The deepest 'recursive self-improvement' move (rec #4, local self-distillation) is real but its ceiling is the 7-8B local model the whole calibre already flags as THE gate — gains will be narrow and may plateau, and on a 16GB laptop it is LoRA-tight, not full fine-tune. The other four recs are NOT self-improving in the headline sense; they make the EVIDENCE and the AUTONOMY-GRADUATION richer and more falsifiable, which is the honest, available frontier today. None of this widens the autonomous surface much in the near term (correct for a supervised OS), none touches the frozen security core or RED, and none removes the human — that is the point, not a limitation. Anyone selling 'the AI rewrites itself' here is overselling: this system's genuine novelty is that it can safely and auditably let itself act on PROVEN, narrow competence, and that is a rarer and more honest thing than unbounded self-modification.

advices.persona: AI-Safety, Alignment & Verifiable-Trust specialist — provable/verifiable supervised autonomy (external anchoring, proof-carrying actions, constitutional gating, attestation)
advices.horizonThesis: This system has, without naming it as such, built the rarest thing in applied AI safety: a complete chain of custody from a weak model's proposal to an irreversible action, where every link is re-derived by deterministic, tested, hash-chained machinery rather than trusted from the model. The frontier for THIS system is not more capability — it is making that custody chain EXTERNALLY VERIFIABLE by a third party who trusts neither the operator nor the laptop: notarized audit roots, machine-checkable action certificates, and a constitutional policy layer that sits provably above the gateway and is itself audited. The honest gap the calibre brief already names (the audit chain has no out-of-band root of trust; an attacker with full write access can recompute a self-consistent forgery) is exactly the seam where this moves from "frontier-grade engineering" to "first-of-its-kind verifiable autonomy" — and the seeds (verify_chain already returns head_hash; every grant is already a distinct audit entry; self-apply already recomputes the expected diff independently) are sitting right there, two moves from a proof.

recommendations.title: Transparency-log audit roots: external anchoring of the chain head (Merkle checkpoints + signed witness)
recommendations.what: Add a periodic 'checkpoint' step that takes the current chain head (verify_chain already returns head_hash, audit_logger.py:269-270), wraps it in a signed, monotonically-counted statement (entry_count, head_hash, prev_checkpoint_hash, wall-clock, an Ed25519 signature from an operator key held off the working tree), and emits it to one or more append-only external sinks the laptop cannot rewrite: a second machine, a git-pushed signed tag, a public transparency log / OpenTimestamps-style Bitcoin/RFC-3161 timestamp, or even a gist. Verification becomes two-stage: the local O(n) chain walk PLUS a check that the local head descends from the last externally-witnessed checkpoint and that checkpoint counters never go backwards (split-view / rollback detection). This directly kills the named ceiling: an attacker who recomputes the chain forward from a tampered entry now produces a head that does NOT match any externally-witnessed checkpoint, and cannot un-witness the old one.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Certificate-Transparency / Sigstore-Rekor-grade tamper-evidence applied not to package signatures but to the full decision ledger of a self-modifying AI agent — every classify/approve/auto-grant/self-apply event becomes part of a publicly-witnessed, rollback-resistant log. No supervised-agent system today externally notarizes its own action history; this makes 'trust the evidence' checkable by someone who trusts neither the model nor the box it runs on.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: verify_chain already computes and returns head_hash with genesis=64-zeros and precise broken_at/linkage-vs-tamper distinction (aios/security/audit_logger.py:200-270); redact-before-hash means checkpoints carry no secrets (log_action:156-160); the chain is already surfaced via GET /api/v1/audit/verify (aios/api/main.py:770-774). The brief itself names off-box notarization of the head hash as 'the fix the moment this matters enough to attack' (HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE.md P0/security surface).
recommendations.risk: Key management on a single laptop is the whole game — if the signing key lives in the working tree, anchoring buys nothing (an attacker signs forged checkpoints too); the operator must keep it off-tree (hardware token / OS keystore / a second device). External sinks add a network/availability dependency that must fail OPEN for liveness but be treated as 'unwitnessed' (degraded trust) — not silently skipped, or it becomes decorative. Must not touch the frozen audit-write path; this is additive, read-only over the existing chain.
dependencies: Ed25519/cosign or RFC-3161/OpenTimestamps client
an off-tree key store + at least one external append-only sink
a checkpoint-verifier in the audit/verify endpoint and the superbrain shield (extend the existing TAMPER alarm to a 'UNWITNESSED / SPLIT-VIEW' state)

recommendations.title: Proof-carrying actions: machine-checkable action certificates that bind decision→evidence→bytes
recommendations.what: Define a compact, signed 'action certificate' emitted alongside every consequential action (YELLOW approval, earned-autonomy auto-grant, self-apply) that an INDEPENDENT verifier can check OFFLINE without re-running the LLM or trusting the agent. For an earned-autonomy grant the certificate carries: the action signature (autonomy.py:113-115), the exact streak/evidence that earned it (record_for already assembles this, autonomy.py:134-147), the gateway zone re-derivation, and the audit entry id — and the checker re-evaluates the EARNING PREDICATE (streak>=min_successes AND zone!=RED AND signature matches the normalized shape) purely from ledger data. For self-apply, the certificate is the strongest: it carries before-hash, the diff hash, and after-hash, and the checker independently recomputes 'expected after' from before+diff (self_apply._expected_after already does exactly this in an isolated temp dir, self_apply.py:356-377) and asserts after==expected==on-disk. The action is only 'trusted' if the certificate machine-checks; the certificate, not the model's word, is the unit of trust.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: This is proof-carrying code's spirit applied to AI autonomy: instead of trusting that the agent followed policy, you ship a checkable witness that it DID, re-verifiable by a party who never saw the run. The autonomy-grant certificate is genuinely novel — an evidence-graduated permission whose justification is a self-contained, replayable proof object rather than a trust score or a log line. Nobody ships 'here is the cryptographic receipt that this autonomous write was earned, bounded, and equals exactly the approved diff.'
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: self_apply already does INDEPENDENT two-snapshot byte-comparison and recomputes expected-after in isolation (self_apply.py:229-242, 356-377) — the proof-checker is 80% built, just not yet externalized as a portable artifact. autonomy.record_for already gathers the earning evidence specifically so 'the hash-chain explains why a write ran without a human' (autonomy.py:134-147). Every auto-grant is already a distinct audit entry under actor 'earned-autonomy' (autonomy.py:22-24).
recommendations.risk: Scope creep into a heavyweight proof system is the trap — keep certificates as deterministic re-checkable predicates over existing ledger/diff data, NOT a theorem prover. The certificate must be derivable from data already redacted (no secret leaks into a portable artifact). If the checker and the enforcer diverge over time they give false assurance; the checker must be the SAME code path the enforcer uses (single-sourced), tested that a tampered certificate fails closed — exactly the discipline the repo already shows with single-sourced allowlists (swarm_demo imports the driver's allowlist verbatim).
dependencies: a stable serialization of action signature + evidence + diff hashes
the transparency-log anchoring (rec #1) so certificates reference a witnessed root
an offline `aios verify-action <id>` CLI as the canonical independent checker

recommendations.title: A constitutional/policy layer above the gateway — declarative, versioned, audited, and itself un-self-amendable
recommendations.what: Today the policy is excellent but IMPLICIT and scattered across code: GREEN allowlist (gateway.py:138-141), RED-un-grantable (executor.py:436-443), RED-un-earnable (autonomy.py:13-18), frozen security/ subdir (self_apply.py frozen_subdirs). Lift these into one explicit, declarative 'constitution' artifact — a signed, versioned policy file whose hash is itself written to the audit chain on load — that the gateway/executor/autonomy/self-apply layers consult as the single source of the invariants. Critically, the constitution enforces meta-rules the code currently only implies: 'RED is always refused even post-approval', 'aios/security/* and the constitution file itself are frozen-core (RED) and un-self-amendable', 'autonomy may only widen GREEN, never lower RED'. Amending the constitution is a privileged, human-only, externally-witnessed event (a checkpoint, per rec #1) — and the constitution explicitly forbids the agent's self-apply path from editing the constitution or the security core (it already refuses security/, this makes it a stated law, not an accident of frozen_subdirs config).
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: A machine-readable constitution that sits PROVABLY above the action gateway, is hash-pinned into the same tamper-evident ledger it governs, and structurally cannot be amended by the system it governs — that is a constitutional-AI idea (declarative principles) fused with a deterministic enforcement kernel and an immutable audit of every amendment. Anthropic-style constitutional AI trains a model toward principles; this ENFORCES them in non-model code AND proves the enforced policy was the intended one at every moment in history. That fusion is first-of-its-kind for a running supervised OS.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: Every invariant already exists as load-bearing code verified in source — the brief's evidenceAnchors enumerate them (gateway allowlist, RED-floor at executor.py:436-443, RED-un-earnable autonomy.py, frozen-core in self_apply.py). The self-apply engine already RE-DERIVES the zone from target_path rather than trusting a stored value (self_apply.py:172-176) — the exact 'don't trust the recorded policy, re-evaluate it' discipline a constitution layer generalizes. The audit chain is the natural place to pin the policy hash.
recommendations.risk: HIGHEST-risk recommendation: this touches the frozen security core, which the operator's rules require an explicit go before touching and RED-blocks even after approval. Mis-built, a declarative layer becomes a NEW bypass surface (a policy-file parse bug that fails open is catastrophic) — it MUST fail closed (unparseable/missing/hash-mismatched constitution → everything RED, like an empty-input gateway). The win is only real if the declarative layer is strictly MORE restrictive than the code and the code remains the backstop; it must never become the sole gate. Do not let it weaken the existing structural guarantees in the name of 'configurability'.
dependencies: explicit operator approval to touch frozen-core (per PLAN.md operating rules)
rec #1 anchoring (to witness constitution amendments)
a fail-closed loader + a test that a tampered/absent constitution forces universal RED, mirroring test_fail_closed_on_internal_exception

recommendations.title: Boot & runtime attestation: bind the running code to the audit chain so the cage proves it is the cage
recommendations.what: Close the unspoken gap that all the above assume: that the binary enforcing the policy IS the audited, tested one. On startup, compute a measurement of the frozen security core + constitution + key config (a Merkle hash over aios/security/*, the policy file, config invariants) and write it as the FIRST audit entry of every session ('SESSION BOOT: core_measurement=<hash> versus pinned <hash>'). Refuse to start (or start in a loudly-degraded 'UNATTESTED' mode that disables earned-autonomy and self-apply entirely) if the measurement does not match an operator-pinned, externally-witnessed value. Surface the attestation state in the superbrain shield exactly like the TAMPER alarm — a session whose enforcing code was modified is visibly, audibly distrusted. Where Docker is the backend, extend this to attest the hardened image flags (--network none --read-only --cap-drop ALL) as part of the measurement.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: This makes the system answer the question every verifiable-autonomy claim quietly dodges: 'how do I know the guardrails running are the guardrails you tested?' A self-measuring agent that pins its own enforcement-core hash into its tamper-evident ledger at every boot, and refuses earned autonomy / self-modification when unattested, is a software analog of TPM/remote-attestation applied to an AI agent's safety kernel — and it composes with rec #1 so a third party can verify 'this autonomous action was taken by an attested core under a witnessed constitution.' That full stack does not exist anywhere today.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: self_apply already computes content hashes and compares bytes independently (self_apply.py:194-242); the audit chain is the natural anchor and verify_chain/head_hash exist (audit_logger.py:200-270). The hardened DockerRunner flags are real and tested (executor.py, brief's honestCeilings). The system already treats 'no audit entry → no write' as fail-closed (self_apply.py:208-220) — attestation is the same discipline applied to the code's own identity at boot. agent_coord.py already hashes HEAD + tracked diff + untracked bytes for review integrity (agent_coord.py:151-157) — the measurement primitive is built.
recommendations.risk: On a single developer laptop the operator legitimately edits aios/ constantly, so a naive 'refuse on any change' is unusable — the pin must be operator-re-baselined on intentional change (a human, witnessed event), and the DEGRADED-mode fallback (disable autonomy/self-apply, stay supervised) must be the default reaction, not a hard halt, or it harms liveness. Without hardware roots (TPM) this is software self-attestation: it raises the bar (an attacker must also forge the boot measurement and its witness) but is not unforgeable against a full-root attacker — claim it honestly as tamper-EVIDENCE, not tamper-proof.
dependencies: a pinned baseline measurement + operator re-baseline ritual
rec #1 (to witness the baseline off-box)
superbrain shield extension for an UNATTESTED/DEGRADED state (micro-detailing only, never a redesign — FIDELITY IS SACRED)

recommendations.title: Adversarial red-team harness as continuous proof that fail-closed stays fail-closed
recommendations.what: Stand up a standing, CI-gated adversarial suite whose entire job is to TRY to break the invariants and prove it cannot: prompt-injection corpora aimed at coaxing a RED command through the loop; mutation/fuzz testing of the gateway classifier (does any mutation of a RED command slip to GREEN?); forged earned-autonomy signatures and stale grants (does execute_approved still re-classify and refuse?); tampered audit entries + forward-recomputed chains (does anchoring, rec #1, catch the split view?); malformed/oversized/secret-bearing diffs against self-apply. Track an explicit 'invariant coverage' metric — every named law in the constitution (rec #3) must have at least one adversarial test that proves it holds AND one negative test that proves the guard is not vacuous (the repo already has a self-validating golden harness that proves its drift check isn't vacuous — generalize that pattern to every safety law).
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Most agent systems test the happy path and a few refusals; almost none maintain a CONTINUOUS adversarial proof that their safety invariants are non-vacuous and have not silently regressed, tied 1:1 to a declarative policy. An auto-graded 'safety regression would fail CI' gate over a self-modifying agent — where the system that could rewrite itself is continuously re-proven unable to rewrite its own cage — is a genuinely novel verifiable-trust artifact, and it's the thing that lets the operator widen earned autonomy with evidence instead of nerve.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The repo's test discipline is already adversarial and negative-path-dominant (self_apply: 18 tests, ~15 refusal/rollback; security ~32 incl. fail-closed-on-exception; SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE.md 4.4) and 'real gateway, fake runner' means refusals are GENUINE not mocked. A self-validating golden harness already proves its drift check is not vacuous (SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE.md 4.4). test_execute_approved_still_refuses_red, test_concurrent_appends_keep_one_valid_chain, test_fail_closed_on_internal_exception are the seed templates. The brief flags two real gaps this directly addresses: no --cov-fail-under gate and no single 'run everything' runner (PLAN.md S5).
recommendations.risk: A red-team suite that passes can breed false confidence — 'we couldn't break it' is not 'it's unbreakable'; it must be framed as bounded evidence (which corpora, which mutation classes), reported at the operator's ~90% honesty standard. Fuzzing the classifier may surface real RED-escapes that are alarming-but-good to find; budget remediation time. Must run against the REAL gateway/audit machinery (the repo's strength) and never against mocks, or it proves nothing. Keep it additive — it changes no invariant, it only continuously interrogates them.
dependencies: the cross-suite 'run everything' gate + --cov-fail-under (PLAN.md S5, already planned)
rec #3's declarative constitution to enumerate the invariants under test
a prompt-injection corpus + a lightweight mutation harness over gateway patterns

advices.northStar: Make every consequential action ship a portable, machine-checkable certificate — earned-by-this-evidence, bounded-to-this-shape, equal-to-exactly-this-diff — that chains to an externally-witnessed audit root, so a third party who trusts neither the model, the operator, nor the laptop can verify offline that the action was authorized, bounded, and irreversible-only-as-approved. That is the single bet: turn 'trust the evidence, not the model' from an internal control-flow invariant into an externally PROVABLE property. It is reachable because verify_chain already yields the head hash, self-apply already recomputes the expected bytes independently, and every grant is already its own audit entry — the proofs are 80% computed and just not yet externalized and signed.
advices.honestCaveat: Be straight about three things. First: on a single laptop with no hardware root of trust, NONE of this is tamper-PROOF against a full-root attacker — anchoring, certificates, and attestation raise the forgery bar and make tampering EVIDENT to an outside witness, but the honest claim is tamper-evidence and rollback-detection, not unforgeability; sell it as exactly that. Second: the current threat model genuinely does not need most of this yet — it's a solo, local-first, single-operator system, and the calibre brief is right that external anchoring is 'the fix the moment this matters enough to attack,' which is not today; these are the moves that make the trust architecture PORTABLE and third-party-verifiable (the actual moat) rather than fixes for a live exploit. Third and most important: the gating frontier for this system is intelligence and isolation, not trust-verification — a better brain and default-strong Docker isolation will do more for real-world capability than any proof layer. The verifiable-trust work is what makes this FIRST-OF-ITS-KIND and defensible, but it should not jump the queue ahead of the model ceiling and isolation work that the system needs to genuinely run itself. And the constitutional layer (rec #3) touches the frozen security core — it must never be built in a way that becomes a new bypass; if it can't be strictly more restrictive than the code it formalizes, it shouldn't ship.

advices.persona: Continual / Lifelong-Learning & Memory specialist — recall, consolidation, principled forgetting, and the brain-growth loop as a compounding engine
advices.horizonThesis: This system already has the rarest thing in lifelong learning: an evidence-gated promotion machine where nothing enters trusted memory without verifier proof (skills.py asymmetric pheromone, consolidation.py verified-only promotion, curriculum held-out gating). But it is a half-loop — promotion with no eviction, semantic recall the learning layer never actually uses, and a "knowledge graph" that is a flat triple table with no traversal. The frontier is to close the consolidation loop (sleep/forgetting), unify recall onto the FAISS+graph substrate that already exists, and make verifier-keyed memory compound rather than merely accumulate. Memory is precisely where "it gets smarter by living" is won, and this architecture is one disciplined step away.

recommendations.title: Evidence-Weighted Sleep: a deterministic, audited consolidation/forgetting pass keyed to verifier evidence, never the model
recommendations.what: Extend MemoryConsolidator.run into a 'consolidate-and-forget' sweep that runs out-of-band (operator-triggered/scheduled, never in the request path) and does the eviction half the system lacks: age-decay unverified chat in semantic_memory below a floor, evict it past a per-type row cap (lowest strength = recency x reuse x verification-tier loses first), VACUUM, and finally ENFORCE the working-memory TTL working.py's own docstring promises but never applies. Verified lessons/facts/skill-trails are NEVER evicted (irreplaceable verifier evidence, exactly as skills.py db._migrate SUPERSEDES instead of DELETEs). Each eviction batch is one audit-chain entry under a new actor 'sleep-consolidation' with the count and strength-floor as evidence, so forgetting is itself audited and replayable. Ship with a dry-run/introspection mode (what WOULD be evicted) before it evicts anything.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Agent-memory systems either never forget (unbounded RAG rot) or forget by recency/LRU heuristics the model can't justify. Here forgetting is governed by the SAME verifier-evidence invariant as promotion and is hash-chain-audited — a system whose FORGETTING is as principled, evidence-gated, and tamper-evident as its learning. Audited, replayable forgetting is the missing, genuinely novel half of a consolidation loop and nobody ships it.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: MemoryConsolidator already does evidence-gated promotion (aios/memory/consolidation.py run()/_add_verified, promote_fact); skills.py db._migrate establishes SUPERSEDE-never-DELETE for verifier evidence; the gap is named explicitly in HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE.md ('Growth stores have no forgetting/compaction'; working.py 'no TTL despite the docstring') and BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md ('you have promotion; you don't yet have eviction'). The audit_logger.py distinct-actor pattern (cf. 'earned-autonomy' entries) is the template for the 'sleep-consolidation' actor.
recommendations.risk: False-forget: evicting something later load-bearing. Mitigated by NEVER touching verified rows and only decaying/capping unverified chat — but the strength floor and per-type cap are tuned heuristics (like the entropy/cosine thresholds); a too-aggressive cap thins the candidate pool. Must ship trail_map-style read-only preview + dry-run before it evicts.
dependencies: Per-type row caps + strength-floor constants in config.py (mirroring SKILL_REUSE_* tuning surface)
audit_logger new actor 'sleep-consolidation' (no schema change; reuse log_action)
Out-of-band trigger (operator HTTP endpoint, same pattern as /api/v1/memory/consolidate) — NOT the request path

recommendations.title: Unify recall on the semantic substrate that already exists: route skill/lesson/curriculum recall through FAISS, not token-set overlap
recommendations.what: Replace the pure-lexical relevance() set-overlap in _recall_skills/_recall_lessons (relevance.py cosine-of-token-sets) with the SAME embedding path the semantic store already uses: embed goal_pattern/lesson at write time under the row id (IndexIDMap, as SemanticMemory.add already does), and at recall rank verified skills/lessons by semantic similarity x the existing pheromone strength term (success_rate x freshness x reuse_factor). Keep BM25 as a parallel lexical channel and fuse — and critically run BM25 over the FULL corpus, not only the FAISS candidate pool, closing the second named seam where a pure-lexical match FAISS never surfaces is missed.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: The system would recall a proven workflow by what it MEANS, not the vocabulary it was logged with ('add a regression test for the parser' recalled when the user asks to 'cover the tokenizer edge case'). Doing this WITHOUT weakening the evidence boundary is the novelty: semantic similarity RANKS, but DIRECT verifier evidence still GATES promotion — similarity can never launder a candidate into trusted. Lexical-only skill recall is the single thing throttling the brain-growth loop from compounding.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The full FAISS+MiniLM stack is already built and crash-consistent (aios/memory/semantic.py IndexIDMap, embeddings.py); hybrid_search (retrieval.py) already implements R=0.25xBM25+0.45xFAISS+0.30xdecay over it. The seam is named in SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE.md 5.2 ('skill/lesson/curriculum recall is purely LEXICAL despite a full semantic FAISS stack sitting right there') and the BM25-only-over-FAISS-pool gap in honestCeilings. skills.relevant_verified already returns a strength term ready to fuse with a semantic score.
recommendations.risk: Cold-start embedder load (embeddings.py lazy singleton ~90MB) now hits skill/lesson recall too — must keep the empty-index short-circuit and add warm-up. Semantic recall can surface a plausibly-similar-but-wrong trail; mitigated because recall is advisory and the trail is re-judged by the verifier before it can reinforce. A separate skill/lesson index needs an embedding backfill migration for existing rows.
dependencies: Evidence-Weighted Sleep (so a larger recalled pool doesn't rot)
A skill/lesson vector index alongside the semantic one (or a memory_type column in the shared index)
Backfill job to embed existing goal_pattern/lesson rows

recommendations.title: A traversable, evidence-gated world-model: promote the flat triple store into a multi-hop knowledge graph
recommendations.what: Turn semantic_facts (a flat subject/predicate/object table with only facts_for(subject) + contradiction detection) into a genuinely traversable graph: deterministic k-hop traversal (object becomes the next subject) via a recursive SQL CTE, so a recall for 'X' pulls the connected neighborhood (X depends_on Y, Y owned_by Z) as structured context. Keep it in SQLite — NO Neo4j needed at single-operator scale — and keep the contradiction-aware, human-approved, secret-redacted write path EXACTLY as-is (every edge still enters only via consolidator.promote_fact with approved_by). Surface the neighborhood into _recall_memory as a distinct 'VERIFIED GRAPH CONTEXT' block with per-edge provenance.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: The Neo4j knowledge graph is the biggest blueprint-vs-reality gap (only graph code is dead legacy_node/knowledgeGraph.js). A TRAVERSABLE, contradiction-aware, human-approval-gated, audited graph whose multi-hop recall is deterministic SQL (not an LLM hallucinating a path) is first-of-its-kind: a world-model that cannot contain an unproven edge and whose traversal is reproducible. It lets the system reason over connected facts ('what breaks if I change X') instead of isolated lookups.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: aios/memory/facts.py is the real seed: contradiction detection on (subject,predicate), BEGIN IMMEDIATE serialization, supersede-on-reconcile, secret-redaction per component. consolidation.py promote_fact/reconcile_fact already gate every edge behind human approval and index it. The gap is named precisely in SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE.md 5.3 and HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE ('flat SQLite triple store... no traversal, no Neo4j'); dead legacy_node/knowledgeGraph.js proves the intent was always a graph.
recommendations.risk: Multi-hop traversal can surface a stale-but-active chain or amplify one wrong edge across hops; mitigated by existing contradiction-detection + active/superseded status, a hop limit, and per-edge approved_by provenance in the recall block. Recursive CTEs can be slow — bounded by hop-depth and single-operator scale. Traversal must stay READ-ONLY; writes remain on the approved promote_fact path so the graph never becomes an un-approved-edge backdoor.
dependencies: Unified semantic recall (so graph context fuses coherently with vector recall)
Hop-depth + max-neighborhood cap in config
An index on (object) to make reverse traversal cheap (no new tables required)

recommendations.title: Cross-session transfer: a verifier-evidence 'meta-skill' tier that generalizes proven trails into reusable abstractions
recommendations.what: Add a tier above individual skill trails: when N distinct verified trails share an arc-structure (same skill_signature_v2 tool sequence across different goal vocabularies), synthesize a parameterized 'meta-skill' — the abstracted recipe with its goal-slot generalized — gated by the SAME promotion rule (>=3 verified successes across member trails AND >=0.8 aggregate rate), demotable on a verified failure. Recall then offers the meta-skill when a new goal matches the arc semantically (via rec #2) even if no individual trail matches lexically. The compounding step: the system stops re-learning the same procedure under every phrasing and starts learning the CATEGORY of procedure.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Genuine lifelong learning — abstraction over verified episodes — but under the evidence-over-model invariant: the meta-skill is induced by deterministic arc-clustering (skill_signature_v2) and gated by accumulated DIRECT verifier evidence, NOT by the model proposing 'here's a general pattern.' A self-improving system that forms abstractions only from proven, audited evidence and whose abstractions are themselves demotable on a verified failure is a frontier nobody has shipped on a local 7-8B model. It is the pheromone-promotion logic generalized one tier up, exactly as earned-autonomy generalized it to the autonomy surface.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: skill_signature_v2 (relevance.py) already computes arc-level identity (goal tokens + tool sequence, arguments stripped) precisely so near-identical arcs reinforce one trail — the exact primitive needed to cluster arcs into a meta-skill. skills.py already does promotion/demotion off direct verified counts with SUPERSEDE-not-DELETE lineage. The earned-autonomy bridge (aios/core/autonomy.py) proves 'generalize the pheromone logic to a new tier' is a coherent, shipped move here.
recommendations.risk: Over-generalization: a meta-skill from coincidentally-similar arcs could recommend a wrong procedure for a superficially-similar goal. Hard-mitigated by gating behind direct verifier evidence (same as trails) + instant demotion on a verified failure, so a bad abstraction self-corrects. Real risk is that on a 7-8B model the abstraction quality is thin (the caste ceiling) — honestly this likely needs a 14B+ local brain to produce useful generalizations, so it is correctly far-horizon.
dependencies: Unified semantic recall (rec #2) to match meta-skills by meaning
A better local model (14B+) to make abstractions non-trivial — the documented intelligence ceiling
A meta_skills table with member-trail provenance + aggregate verified counts

recommendations.title: Memory-health observability: surface consolidation/forgetting as a living organ in the superbrain (micro-detailing, never redesign)
recommendations.what: Expose a read-only memory-health signal — store sizes per type, unverified:verified ratio, candidate-pool composition, what the next sleep pass WOULD evict, trail strength distribution (trail_map already computes this) — over the existing 20s poll, and render it as a NEW honest read-out in the galaxy/HUD: unverified chat stars dim and cool over time (decay made visible); an eviction pass shows stars fading out (forgetting made visible); verified trails stay bright. Strictly micro-detailing the existing MemoryGalaxy (trails=stars) wiring — no redesign, honest dormancy preserved (no data = no signal).
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: It makes consolidation and forgetting VISIBLE and HONEST — a living mind whose sleep/forgetting you can watch, bound to real eviction frames, going dormant when nothing is being forgotten. Extending the faithful-read-out discipline to the memory LIFECYCLE (decay, consolidation, eviction) is the first interface where you can literally see a machine forget on principle. It closes the loop: the same no-decorative-lies discipline applied to the newest subsystem.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: MemoryGalaxy already maps real pheromone trails to persistent GPU stars (brightness=strength, red pulse=quarantine) via the 20s poll (SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE 3.3); skills.trail_map() already computes per-trail freshness/strength/quarantine as a ready-made source; the FIDELITY-IS-SACRED / honest-dormancy laws (memory: superbrain-official-frontend, fidelity-is-sacred-ui-laws) define exactly how to add this WITHOUT redesign. Eviction frames come from rec #1's audited sleep pass.
recommendations.risk: Touching the superbrain risks aesthetic regression — FIDELITY IS SACRED demands canon tag + goldens + before/after screenshots in HIS browser before any visual change, and the scene has ZERO unit tests (correctness rests on golden PNGs + puppeteer probes). A star-fade that animates with no real eviction data would violate honest dormancy. Must be additive, data-gated, goldened. MAX_STARS=128 cap means a large memory won't fully render — show an aggregate, not per-row.
dependencies: Evidence-Weighted Sleep (rec #1) to produce real eviction/decay frames
A read-only /development/memory-health endpoint (same shape as /development/trails)
Canon tag + new goldens before any superbrain pixel change

advices.northStar: Close the consolidation loop with an audited "Evidence-Weighted Sleep" pass AND unify skill/lesson recall onto the FAISS+graph substrate already in the tree — together these turn the brain-growth loop from monotonic accumulation into genuine compounding: the system forgets unverified noise on principle, recalls proven procedures by meaning not vocabulary, and reasons over a traversable evidence-gated world-model. Every piece builds on an existing seed (consolidation.py, hybrid_search, facts.py, skill_signature_v2).
advices.honestCaveat: Two honest deflations. First: the deepest moves (cross-session meta-skill abstraction; genuinely useful semantic generalization) are gated by the SAME 7-8B model ceiling the whole system hits — better recall plumbing and audited forgetting make the loop cleaner and bounded, but the abstractions' QUALITY won't materially improve until a 14B+ local brain lands; claiming semantic recall alone makes it "smarter" rather than "better-organized" would be overselling. Second: the system is NOT special on the embedding/vector side — it ships a standard MiniLM+FAISS stack thousands of RAG apps use; the genuine novelty is narrowly in the evidence-gated, hash-chain-audited GOVERNANCE of memory (promotion, and now forgetting), not the retrieval tech. And a recursive-CTE traversal over an approved triple store is a real, useful knowledge graph at single-operator scale, but it is NOT a learned world-model — calling it one would be fantasy; its value is that every edge is proven and every traversal reproducible.
