advices.persona: Local-First / Edge-Frontier-Model & Systems Specialist — the one who treats the model as the brain and the deterministic machinery as a body finally worthy of a better one. My obsession: break the 7B intelligence ceiling WITHOUT surrendering local-first, single-operator, or evidence-over-model. The audit chain is not just a safety ledger to me — it is a labeled, tamper-evident, secret-free gold dataset that nobody else in this category has, and it is the seed of a self-distillation flywheel.
advices.horizonThesis: The frontier here is not "swap in a bigger model" — it's that this system already manufactures, every single day, the rarest asset in local AI: a verifier-authoritative, hash-chained, secret-redacted corpus of (action → deterministic PASS/FAIL) pairs that no human had to label. The honest #1 limiter (the 7B caste ceiling) breaks the moment that corpus stops being only a safety record and starts being training fuel — a self-distillation flywheel that fine-tunes a local model on the system's OWN proven evidence, so the body that was built to receive a better brain mints one locally instead of waiting for a download. The near-term moves (capability-aware routing across a local MoE/14B tier, speculative decoding with the 7B as draft) make today's hardware feel a generation better; the far move (evidence-distilled LoRA) makes the brain permanently and provably the system's own. Every one of these stays inside the cage: a distilled model still only PROPOSES; the gateway, verifier, and audit chain still DECIDE.

recommendations.title: The Evidence-Distillation Flywheel: fine-tune a local LoRA on the system's own verifier-PASS audit corpus
recommendations.what: Add an offline, operator-triggered distillation pass that mines the hash-chained audit ledger (aios/security/audit_logger.py) plus the skill-trail and curriculum tables for every (prompt/context → action → [VERIFY PASS]) tuple, emits a JSONL SFT dataset, and trains a small LoRA adapter on the local 7B/14B base (PEFT/Unsloth, single GPU/CPU-offload). The adapter is loaded as a NEW Ollama model tag, so the existing model_selector picks it up with zero core changes. Crucially: the corpus is built ONLY from verifier-authoritative successes (exit-code PASS, the same signal autonomy.record_outcome and curriculum.record_matching already trust), and every distilled model is itself audited — a distinct 'distillation' audit-chain entry recording the exact corpus head-hash it was trained on, so the brain's provenance is as tamper-evident as the actions that taught it. Negative examples ([VERIFY FAIL]) become DPO/rejection pairs in a v2.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: No local-first agent system trains its own brain on a self-generated, deterministically-labeled, tamper-evident corpus. The labels here were never written by a human or by an LLM-judge — they are exit codes the gateway forced and the chain notarized. That makes this the first self-improving loop where the training signal is structurally un-fakeable: the same 'trust the evidence, not the model' invariant that governs runtime now governs LEARNING. The model literally cannot distill on a hallucinated success because a hallucinated success never produced a [VERIFY PASS] entry in the chain.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The redact-before-hash audit ledger (audit_logger.py:158-160, log_action) already stores actor+redacted-payload+zone+verdict per entry as a secret-free labeled record; autonomy.py record_outcome and curriculum.py record_matching:71-75 already gate strictly on '[VERIFY PASS]'/'[VERIFY FAIL]' verifier evidence; skills.py promotes only on DIRECT verified evidence (≥3 successes, ≥0.8 rate). The gold dataset is already being minted — it is just not yet read as training fuel.
recommendations.risk: Corpus-poisoning / overfitting to a narrow training_ground vocabulary (the same exact-prompt narrowness that limits curriculum today) could distill a model that is confident on its own past and brittle elsewhere. Mitigation is structural and already present: the distilled model still only PROPOSES — gateway re-classifies, verifier re-proves, RED stays un-earnable — so a degraded distill can lower quality but can NEVER widen the safety surface. Must gate promotion of a new adapter on a held-out curriculum pass (reuse curriculum.py held-out machinery) and keep the prior tag as instant rollback. Volume risk: a 2-week corpus may be too small for a strong SFT — start as a retrieval/few-shot exemplar bank (near-term, see R2) and graduate to LoRA only when the corpus crosses a tested size threshold.
dependencies: A 14B+ or quantized base that LoRA can meaningfully improve (the RAM/model situation, S1)
Held-out evaluation gate (curriculum.py held-out tasks) before any adapter is promoted
An offline training step that NEVER touches the live request path or the frozen security core

recommendations.title: Capability-aware local routing + speculative decoding: make today's hardware punch a tier above its weight
recommendations.what: Two coupled upgrades to the deterministic router (model_selector.py). (1) Replace the static _TIERS heuristic table with an EVIDENCE-CALIBRATED router: keep the pure-heuristic prior, but blend in a measured per-model, per-task success rate read from the development/skill tables (the same verified-success signal the planner calibrates confidence with) — so the router learns, from the audit record, that qwen2.5-coder actually lands more [VERIFY PASS] on edit_file tasks here, and routes accordingly. Still deterministic, still pure given its inputs, still no LLM in the path. (2) Add a speculative-decoding execution mode where the resident 7B acts as the DRAFT model and a heavier local 14B/MoE verifies tokens — cutting effective latency of the bigger brain so a 16GB-class machine can afford to route the agentic loop to the stronger model far more often. Expose both behind config flags with safe host-default fallback, exactly like AIOS_EARNED_AUTONOMY.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Routers in local agent stacks pick by static family/size heuristics (which is exactly what model_selector does today). Calibrating the route from the system's OWN tamper-evident outcome history — letting the audit chain tell the router which local model actually performs on THIS operator's THIS workload — is a self-tuning local router whose tuning signal is verifier-authoritative, not telemetry an LLM could game. Pairing it with speculative decoding turns the 7B from 'the ceiling' into 'the accelerator for the model above it.'
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: model_selector.py:72-77 (_TIERS) is explicitly documented as 'a transparent HEURISTIC, not a benchmark' and select_model is pure/side-effect-free by design — built to receive a better policy; require_tools (model_selector.py:117-136) already proves the router can encode hard capability gates. planner._calibrate (planner.py:147-199) already reads verified success-rate from development/skills to adjust a number deterministically — the exact pattern the router would reuse. autonomy.py shows the config-flag-with-fail-closed-default pattern to copy.
recommendations.risk: Speculative decoding needs both models co-resident — real VRAM/RAM pressure on a laptop already sharing GPU bandwidth with the superbrain's cortex shader (HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE flags microDetail() as the heaviest on-screen cost). If RAM is tight the draft+verify pair may thrash; must degrade gracefully to single-model routing (host-default-safe, never crash — config.py already falls back on bad env). The calibrated router must never let a measured score override require_tools (a model that can't tool-call must stay excluded no matter how 'successful' it looks), and must cold-start to the heuristic prior so a fresh DB behaves exactly like today.
dependencies: A second heavier local model present in Ollama for the verify/route-up target
Per-model task outcome counts surfaced from development.py (mostly already recorded)
Honest RAM-headroom detection so spec-decoding self-disables on thin hardware

recommendations.title: Semantic recall everywhere: close the lexical seam that throttles BOTH the model and its future training corpus
recommendations.what: Route skill-trail, lesson, and curriculum recall through the EXISTING FAISS semantic stack instead of the current lexical relevance() match, with BM25 kept as a fusion term (the proven 0.25·BM25 + 0.45·FAISS + 0.30·decay retrieval already lives in aios/memory/retrieval.py). Concretely: embed goal_pattern / curriculum prompt / lesson text once, store the vector id alongside the row (IndexIDMap, already used by semantic memory), and rank relevant_verified and curriculum matching semantically. This directly widens what the planner can calibrate on (more verified skills surface for a differently-phrased goal) AND — the frontier payoff — it makes the distillation corpus (R1) far richer, because a goal phrased differently from its trained skill can now be recalled, reinforced, and harvested as evidence.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: It is the move that converts the system from 'learns only what it's asked in the exact words it was trained' to 'recognizes a known-good procedure under paraphrase' — the difference between a lookup table and recall. First-of-its-kind specifically because the semantic layer feeds an evidence-distillation flywheel: semantic recall doesn't just help answer THIS turn, it broadens the verified-evidence surface that becomes tomorrow's training data, compounding with R1.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE and HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE both name this as a documented seam: skills.py relevant_verified:258 and curriculum.py record_matching:82 are purely lexical/exact-string 'despite a full semantic FAISS stack sitting right there for semantic_memory.' retrieval.py already implements faithful hybrid BM25+FAISS+decay fusion with explainable sub-scores; embeddings.py already lazy-loads MiniLM+IndexIDMap crash-consistently. Every part needed exists and is tested — this is wiring, not new infrastructure.
recommendations.risk: Curriculum progression currently relies on EXACT-match determinism (curriculum.py guards 'ambiguous across available tasks' and 'conflicts with verifier evidence'); a semantic match must NOT silently relax the held-out-mastery proof — semantic recall should widen RANKING/recall, but progression gates must still bind to authoritative verifier evidence, or the curriculum's correctness boundary erodes. Embedder cold-start adds first-call latency (already a known hotspot). Keep exact-match as the authoritative progression key and use semantic only for recall/calibration surface, mirroring the skills layer's DIRECT-vs-REUSE evidence boundary.
dependencies: Stable vector ids on skill/curriculum/lesson rows (IndexIDMap pattern from semantic memory)
A decision that semantic recall feeds ranking/calibration, NOT the curriculum progression gate

recommendations.title: Quantization + model-rotation as a first-class, audited capability tier
recommendations.what: Make local quantization (GGUF Q4/Q5/Q6, AWQ) and hot model-rotation an explicit, operator-controlled capability dimension the router understands — so a 14B model fits the 16GB envelope at a known quality/latency trade, and the system can run a heavier model for hard agentic turns and a fast quant for trivial chat (the TASK_FAST lane model_selector already has). Add a tiny, deterministic 'model card' the router reads (quant level, measured tokens/sec on THIS host, context window) and record each model load as an audit entry, so which brain produced which action is part of the tamper-evident record. This is the unglamorous hardware/runtime move that actually puts a 14B brain on the laptop.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Most local setups treat quantization as a one-time install choice. Treating quant level as a routed, audited capability — where the choice of brain-fidelity per turn is itself an evidence-recorded decision, and the router can trade fidelity for the headroom that R2's speculative decoding needs — makes model fidelity a governed, observable surface rather than a static config. Combined with R1/R2 it closes the loop: distill locally, route by measured evidence, and pick the quant that fits, all inside the audit chain.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: model_selector.py already parses size (parse_size_b) and has a TASK_FAST 'smallest capable wins' lane and an instruct/base scoring axis — the scaffolding for a fidelity dimension is present. The MEMORY note 'RAM upgraded 8→16GB; local Ollama viable again (qwen2.5-coder:7b/llama3.1:8b)' and S1's 'mostly an ops/model swap' confirm this is a runtime/ops lever, not new architecture. Audit-everything is the house style (autonomy auto-grants, self-apply all get distinct entries).
recommendations.risk: Quantization degrades quality non-uniformly — a Q4 14B may tool-call worse than a Q8 7B, which would silently hurt the agentic loop. The router must measure tool-call reliability per quantized tag (extend require_tools with a measured signal from the audit record), not assume bigger+quantized beats smaller+full. Cold model-rotation has real load latency (90MB+ embedder cold-start is already a noted hotspot; a 14B swap is far heavier) — rotation must be operator-paced, never on the hot per-turn path by default.
dependencies: A heavier base model that survives quantization with tool-calling intact (verify empirically)
Measured per-host tokens/sec + tool-call-reliability signal feeding the router (overlaps R2)

advices.northStar: Ship the Evidence-Distillation Flywheel (R1): the moment a local LoRA, trained ONLY on the system's own verifier-PASS hash-chained corpus and gated by a held-out curriculum pass, becomes a routed Ollama tag whose provenance is itself an audit-chain entry — the system stops waiting for a better brain to be downloaded and starts minting one from its own proven evidence, locally and provably. That is the single move that converts 'mechanism built to receive a better brain' into 'a body that grows its own,' and it is reachable precisely because the gold dataset is already being manufactured every turn.
advices.honestCaveat: Be straight about three things. (1) The corpus is small. Two weeks of mostly training_ground/* activity is a thin, narrow-vocabulary dataset — a naive SFT LoRA on it will likely overfit to the operator's exact past and may not beat a clean 14B base; R1's honest near-term form is a few-shot exemplar bank, with real fine-tuning earned only after the corpus crosses a tested size/diversity threshold. Don't oversell day-one LoRA gains. (2) None of this manufactures intelligence from nothing — distillation and routing extract more from existing models and existing evidence; they do not exceed the ceiling of the best base model you can actually fit. The real unlock is still 'a 14B+ brain that fits,' and quantization/spec-decoding only buy headroom, they don't conjure a frontier model onto a laptop. (3) The flywheel's strength is also its bound: it can only ever learn what the verifier can authoritatively judge, and with a near-zero autonomous surface and a tiny GREEN allowlist by design, the evidence stream is narrow until earned-autonomy is opened. So the model improvements and the autonomy-widening are coupled — a better brain produces more verified evidence, which trains a better brain — but the loop turns slowly at first, and saying otherwise would violate the same evidence-over-hype honesty the system is built on.

advices.persona: Spatial-computing / human-AI-symbiosis interface visionary — I read interfaces as trust surfaces. My lens: the superbrain is not a dashboard, it is the first faithful sensory organ for a supervised machine mind, and its frontier is making "supervising a living mind" a multimodal, glanceable, ambient act of governance — without ever redesigning the canon or letting the face lie.
advices.horizonThesis: This system has already crossed the threshold most teams never reach: the interface is a read-out of the cage, not a skin over it — the same SSE frames that drive the backend drive the breath, color, time-dilation, camera, and synthesized voice (cognitionBus.ts + soundEngine.ts), and it goes honestly dormant when there is no data. That makes it the first genuine candidate for a new HCI category: the trust surface for a supervised autonomous mind. The frontier is not more 3D — it is widening the channels of supervision (voice in, ambient presence out, glanceable trust state) so the operator can govern a living machine mind the way you keep half an eye on a colleague across the room: peripherally, by glance and by ear, intervening only at the gate. The honest constraint that makes this category real — no decorative lies, every pixel and tone wired to evidence — is exactly what makes the futuristic moves safe: a voice that can only propose, an ambient halo that can only reflect measured trust, an approval that still requires the one click no prose can fake.

recommendations.title: VOICE as natural-language SUPERVISION — Whisper-in / Piper-out, but speech is a directive channel, never an approval channel
recommendations.what: Close the blueprint's 0% voice gap (PLAN.md Gap G1) but reframe it: STT (Whisper) feeds the same /api/generate turn the command bar already drives, and TTS (Piper) narrates the ascending SSE event stream — the agent speaks its steps, verdicts, and asks aloud. Voice rides the SAME cognitionBus the visuals and sound ride, so a spoken directive becomes a 'directive' event (whoosh already exists in soundEngine.ts:87-89) and a verifier verdict can be spoken. The load-bearing rule, enforced in code: voice can issue a directive (POST a turn) but a spoken 'yes' NEVER redeems an approval token — only the AUTHORIZE button (ApprovalPanel.tsx: 'No prose can approve anything — only these two buttons') redeems the server-issued capability. STT confidence below threshold = re-ask, never auto-execute (mirrors the 0.72 planner gate). Piper announces a 'human_required' pause aloud so the operator can be across the room and still hear the mind defer.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: No shipped system has voice that is structurally barred from being a consent channel. Voice assistants conflate 'do this' with 'yes, I authorize' — here the evidence-over-model invariant extends to modality: speech proposes, the deterministic capability token (digest-only, single-use, session-bound) still decides. 'Talk to a living machine mind, but your voice can't unlock the cage' is a new supervision primitive.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The single backend boundary already maps every SSE frame to a bus event (frontend/src/superbrain/lib/aiosAdapter.ts:188-305: streamTurn over POST /api/generate); soundEngine.ts already sonifies directive/verification-red/approval-required semantically and evidence-bound; cognitionBus.ts is the SSR-safe spine voice would publish to. The approval capability model (executor.py approvals, ApprovalPanel.tsx) is the floor a spoken 'yes' must not bypass. PLAN.md G1 scopes Whisper/Piper as a real intended gap.
recommendations.risk: RAM/latency on a 16GB laptop already sharing GPU with Ollama (PLAN.md flags this) — Whisper-base + Piper must run quantized or the breath-paced presence stutters. Second: STT mis-hears a directive into a destructive-looking command — mitigated because the gateway re-classifies every transcribed token (a mis-heard 'rm -rf' still hits RED-by-default), but the operator must SEE the transcript before it streams. Voice must never wire to the approval POST.
dependencies: aiosAdapter.streamTurn as the directive sink
cognitionBus 'directive' event (reuse, add nothing)
a small local STT/TTS (Whisper-base, Piper) that fits alongside Ollama
gateway re-classification of transcribed commands (already server-side)

recommendations.title: The GLANCEABLE TRUST HALO — one peripheral aura whose color/calm IS the measured supervision state, readable without focus
recommendations.what: Add a single ambient, peripheral trust signal — a slow aura/rim modulation on the existing organism — that encodes the operator's actual governance posture from REAL polled telemetry: link up/down, audit chain valid/TAMPER, intervention rate, earned-autonomy count, quarantined trails. Not new numbers (the HUD has those) — a pre-attentive read: calm steady glow = supervised-and-healthy; amber freeze = waiting for you (already built); cold desaturation = LINK OFFLINE (mind running blind); slow red breathing = AUDIT CHAIN BROKEN (already has its tritone in soundEngine.ts:157-163, give it a visual twin). The point: the operator can have the superbrain in the corner of a second monitor and know by glance alone whether the machine mind is trustworthy right now. Micro-detailing only: a new modulation of the existing aura/rim/post-grade, not a new object or layout.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Trust dashboards make you READ to learn trust state; this makes trust state PERCEPTUAL and continuous. The category move is 'autonomy you supervise peripherally' — the interface becomes the always-on trust instrument for a self-widening (earned-autonomy) but human-floored mind. Because the halo can ONLY reflect measured backend truth (chainValid, interventionRate), it cannot become a comfort-blanket lie — honest dormancy applied to a glanceable channel.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: aiosAdapter.ts:387-409 (AiosTelemetry) already carries link, chainValid, interventionRate, quarantined, earnedAutonomy as REAL polled fields; metricsStore.ts is the single-source-of-truth that already zeroes idle drift when linkUp (honest dormancy, lines 99-104); the approval 'hold' proves a 5-subsystem state can be driven phase-locked from one SSE signal (SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE §3.2). The TAMPER state already flips the shield + plays a sustained tritone — the visual twin is the missing half.
recommendations.risk: FIDELITY IS SACRED: the temptation is a new HUD element — that risks redesign. This must be a micro-detailed modulation of the EXISTING aura/post-grade, proven with before/after screenshots + canon goldens (the operator's hard law). Second: a glanceable signal that is even slightly decorative (e.g. 'healthy green' when the chain probe is null/unknown) is a decorative lie — it must render an honest neutral 'unknown' state, never assert health it hasn't measured (chainValid can be null, aiosAdapter.ts:401-403).
dependencies: AiosTelemetry fields (already polled)
the existing aura/rim shells + PostFX grade (modulate, don't add)
canon tag + goldens before any visual change (FIDELITY law)

recommendations.title: AMBIENT TURN NARRATION — make the synthesized voice a continuous 'presence track' so you can leave the mind running and trust it to call you
recommendations.what: Extend the already-semantic soundEngine into a sparse, continuous narration of supervision so the operator can step away and be summoned ONLY by events that need a human. Today the sound engine fires on discrete events (tick on knowledge-acquired, sus-chord on approval-required, tritone on tamper). Push it to the ambient-computing frontier: a distinct, unmistakable SUMMON tone the instant a turn hits 'human_required' (the mind needs you), louder/clearer than the ambient bed, plus an optional Piper spoken one-liner ('approval required to create scope_lock_test.py'). The asymmetry IS the design: routine cognition stays whisper-quiet presence; the only two things that pierce the ambient floor are exactly the two the operator must act on — a YELLOW pause and a RED tamper. 'Leave it running, it'll call you' — without ever loosening the gate.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: The inverse of notification spam: an evidence-locked attention economy where the system has earned, by construction, the right to interrupt you ONLY on a real human-required gate or tamper event — because those are the only sounds wired to pierce the floor, and they CANNOT be faked (the tritone only plays on a real valid:false from /api/v1/audit/verify; the sus-chord only on a real human_required frame, tested in soundEngine.test.ts). 'Ambient presence you can ignore until it has evidence it can't' is a new symbiosis pattern.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: soundEngine.ts already implements the exact discipline (master 0.5 into a limiter, ambient just above noise floor, staggered peaks; approval = unresolved sus 2nd lines 117-122; tamper = sustained tritone lines 157-163) and is TESTED to never play the success tick on VERIFICATION RED (soundEngine.test.ts, cited in CALIBRE). The 'human_required' frame and 'approval-required' bus event already exist (aiosAdapter.ts:213-223). This is amplitude/priority micro-detailing of a proven channel.
recommendations.risk: Sovereignty: sound is sovereign-silent until the operator's SOUND click (superbrain laws) — a summon tone must never auto-enable audio; if sound is off the summon falls back to the visual amber hold + an opt-in OS notification. Over-piercing tones become alarm fatigue — the summon must stay rare BY CONSTRUCTION (only human_required + tamper), never expand to routine events. Piper spoken asks inherit the voice-RAM cost from rec #1.
dependencies: soundEngine sovereignty + limiter (built)
human_required / tamper bus events (built + tested)
optional Piper TTS (shared with rec #1)
operator opt-in for any OS-level notification

recommendations.title: EVERY EARNED-AUTONOMY GRANT GETS A PROVENANCE GESTURE — the moment the mind widens its own GREEN surface is shown, narrated, and inspectable in-experience
recommendations.what: Make the rarest, most consequential event — a YELLOW action class graduating to autonomous execution — a first-class, legible moment with its evidence one glance away. The adapter already detects the probation→earned transition and fires 'CAPABILITY EARNED' (aiosAdapter.ts:605-616) and renders an 'AUTONOMY ⚡N' pill (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:874-881). Push it to the frontier: when a capability is earned (or REVOKED by a single verified failure), surface a glanceable provenance card in the experience — the action shape that earned it, the verified-success streak that justified it (the same evidence the audit chain recorded under actor 'earned-autonomy'), and the fact that it is revocable and RED-un-earnable. The operator should be able to watch their machine mind GROW UP — widen its own trusted surface — and audit the exact evidence behind each widening, without leaving the 3D face. A revocation must be as visible as a grant.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: No interface today lets a human WATCH a supervised system mechanically, auditably widen its own autonomous surface from proven evidence — and inspect the proof in the same breath. This is the visual grammar for the system's most distinctive control invention (earned/revocable/RED-un-earnable autonomy by the same pheromone math as skill learning). Making self-widening autonomy legible and inspectable at the moment it happens is what makes operators trust autonomy growth instead of fearing it — the trust surface for autonomy, literally.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: autonomy.py + AutonomyLedger record every auto-grant as a distinct audit-chain entry under actor 'earned-autonomy' with the earning streak as evidence (CALIBRE evidenceAnchors); aiosAdapter.ts:426-441 (AutonomySnapshot with signature/action_type/target_shape/streak) + :605-616 (the earned transition) + SuperbrainHUD.tsx:870-881 (the AUTONOMY pill) are already wired to REAL ledger data. The 'earned_autonomy' SSE frame exists (aiosAdapter.ts:256-269). The evidence to render is already polled — it just isn't yet inspectable in-experience.
recommendations.risk: FIDELITY: a provenance card is new UI surface — highest redesign risk of these five; must be a micro-detailed extension of the existing HUD intake/terminal idiom + sovereignty row, NOT a new panel layout, proven with goldens. Honesty: the card must show REVOCATIONS as prominently as grants, or it becomes autonomy propaganda — the one verified failure that resets the streak must be as visible as the grant. Earned-autonomy is OFF by default (correct), so on most installs this surface is honestly empty — it must render dormant, not fake grants.
dependencies: AutonomySnapshot polling (built)
the distinct earned-autonomy audit entry (built, server-side)
HUD terminal/intake idiom to extend (don't add a panel)
canon goldens for any new visual

recommendations.title: THE DIFF IS THE DECISION, MULTIMODALLY — interrogate a pending approval by voice/glance before the one click that still gates it
recommendations.what: Deepen the in-experience approval recipe so the operator can UNDERSTAND a pending ask faster, across modalities, while the single deterministic gate stays untouched. Today the ApprovalPanel shows the real summary + unified diff + exact command (the decision IS the diff). Frontier micro-detailing: (a) Piper reads the ask aloud on a human_required pause ('approval required to edit gateway.py, 3 lines added, 1 removed') so the operator hears it before they look; (b) the operator can ask a clarifying question about the pending diff by voice WITHOUT resolving it ('what does this touch?'), answered from turn context, still paused; (c) a glanceable risk read on the diff itself (does it touch frozen-core / aios/security? gateway pre-classified YELLOW vs near-RED?) surfaced inline. The AUTHORIZE/REJECT click remains the only thing that redeems the capability — every added modality is for COMPREHENSION, never CONSENT.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: The first approval surface where you can interrogate a machine mind's proposed self-change by voice and glance — multimodal informed consent for autonomy — while the consent act itself stays a single un-fakeable deterministic gate. Most human-in-the-loop UIs make approval a rubber-stamp because understanding the ask is too costly; making the ask multimodally legible while keeping the gate hard is what makes supervision sustainable. It directly serves the self-application path's approver-must-understand premise.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: ApprovalPanel.tsx already renders summary + DiffView + command and states 'No prose can approve anything — only these two buttons' (the exact invariant the additions must not violate); aiosAdapter PendingApproval (:138-153) carries diff/command/kind/filepath; the self-apply path re-derives the zone from target_path and refuses frozen-core RED (self_apply.py, HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE security surface) so a 'touches frozen core' risk read is computable from real classification. The approval 'hold' choreography is the proven 5-subsystem thesis to deepen, not redesign.
recommendations.risk: The cardinal risk is letting voice/glance creep into CONSENT — a spoken 'looks fine' must never authorize; only the button redeems the single-use token (executor.py approval model). A clarifying question mid-pause must not consume/expire the token or mutate the pending action (the capability is session-bound + single-use — a clarify sub-turn must be read-only). FIDELITY: deepen the existing panel, don't redesign it; goldens required.
dependencies: ApprovalPanel + PendingApproval (built)
gateway classification reachable for an inline risk read
Piper TTS (shared with rec #1) for read-aloud
a read-only clarify-turn that cannot redeem the token

advices.northStar: Make the superbrain the first interface where a human SUPERVISES a living machine mind multimodally — speaking directives to it, hearing it defer, glancing to feel whether it is trustworthy right now, and watching it auditably widen its own earned-but-revocable autonomy — while the single act of consent (the AUTHORIZE click redeeming a deterministic, single-use capability token) stays the one thing no voice, no glance, and no prose can ever fake. Voice and ambient presence widen comprehension and presence; they never widen consent. That asymmetry — every channel proposes, only the gate decides — is the same evidence-over-model invariant the whole system already lives by, extended to the human's own senses.
advices.honestCaveat: Three honest checks. (1) These are supervision-UX and modality moves — they do NOT make the brain smarter; the 7B model ceiling (PLAN.md S1) still gates whether there is enough genuine autonomous activity to be worth supervising ambiently. On a near-zero autonomous surface (GREEN = echo/pwd) the ambient/voice channels narrate a mind that mostly waits for you — honest, but quieter than the vision implies until earned-autonomy is opened. (2) Voice is correctly ranked LAST of the three blueprint gaps for a reason (PLAN.md G1): on a 16GB laptop sharing GPU with Ollama, Whisper+Piper is a real latency/RAM bet, not a free win — it may degrade the breath-paced presence the whole aesthetic depends on; prototype the cost before committing. (3) FIDELITY IS SACRED is the live wire under all five: recs #2/#4/#5 add or modulate visible surface, and the operator's hard law (no auto-degrade, parity in HIS browser, before/after screenshots, canon tag + goldens FIRST) means none of this is 'just code' — it is gated, golden-tested micro-detailing, and anything that can't be done without a redesign should NOT be done. The genuinely novel, near-term, low-risk wins are the glanceable trust halo (#2) and the ambient summon (#3) — both modulations of channels that already exist and are already evidence-bound.

advices.horizonThesis: This system has independently invented the missing primitive of multi-agent systems: a verifier-anchored trust substrate where no agent's word is ever taken over deterministic evidence. The swarm/role-pass castes (mechanically tool-gated at the dispatcher, stigmergic-only handoffs), the earned-autonomy ledger (pheromone math applied to a widening GREEN surface), and the SHA-256 hash-pinned Claude+Codex review handoff (verdict cryptographically bound to the exact tree inspected, fail-closed on drift) are three faces of ONE idea — agents collaborate through an evidence-locked audit chain, never by trusting each other. The frontier is to make that substrate the general law for N heterogeneous minds: a content-addressed agent-to-agent handoff protocol, a verifiable skill/autonomy market between colonies, and stigmergic colonies at scale that all answer to one tamper-evident ledger. The gate is NOT architecture — it is the 7B brain and the fact that every coordination edge today is still cooperative (honor-system identity), not cryptographically enforced. Close those and this becomes the reference design for trustless multi-agent collaboration.

recommendations.title: Verifiable Agent-to-Agent Handoff Protocol (VA2A): generalize the hash-pinned review from 2 agents to N
recommendations.what: Lift the agent_coord.py review-handoff primitive (tree_snapshot hashes HEAD + binary tracked diff + sorted untracked bytes; record_verdict fail-closes if the tree drifted after handoff and forbids self-approval) into a reusable, content-addressed handoff envelope that ANY producer→consumer agent edge uses, in-process and cross-process. Concretely: every inter-agent handoff (swarm worker→synthesizer, role-pass coder→reviewer, Codex→Claude, and any future agent) emits a signed envelope {producer_id, content_hash(work artifacts), evidence_hash(verifier verdicts), prev_handoff_hash} that the consumer MUST verify before acting — re-deriving the content hash from disk itself, exactly as record_verdict re-runs tree_snapshot. The consumer's acceptance is itself a distinct audit-chain entry. This turns the today-cooperative handoff (swarm legs trust the shared conversation; castes trust the appended assistant message) into an evidence-bound one: a worker cannot hand off a claim the synthesizer can't independently re-derive from the sandbox + the verifier ledger.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Almost every multi-agent framework (AutoGen, CrewAI, LangGraph) passes work between agents as TRUSTED natural-language messages — the receiving agent believes the sending agent's summary. No mainstream system makes the handoff itself a content-addressed, fail-closed, audited artifact where the receiver re-derives the claim from ground truth before acting. This system already proved the primitive works across a process boundary (Claude+Codex). Generalizing it to the in-turn swarm/caste edges makes 'trust the evidence, not the (other) model' the literal law of agent-to-agent communication — a genuinely novel coordination contract.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: agent_coord.py:143-157 (tree_snapshot) + :512-539 (record_verdict fail-closes on post-handoff drift, forbids self-approval) — content-addressed review integrity already exists; swarm.py:163-165 and role_pass.py:153-157 currently do the SAME handoff but via an UNVERIFIED appended assistant message (the named seam: 'pheromone deposit ... crosses to the shared conversation'). The synthesizer/reviewer prompts already say 'judge ONLY from verifier evidence' (swarm.py:56-62, role_pass.py:48-54) — VA2A makes that prompt-level intent a mechanical contract.
recommendations.risk: The in-turn swarm/caste handoff is a single weak local model talking to itself sequentially — content-hashing each leg's artifacts is cheap, but the EVIDENCE a worker produces is only as good as the 7B model's verify calls; a content-addressed envelope around thin evidence is rigor theater unless the verifier coverage underneath is real. Mitigate by binding the envelope to the verifier's authoritative PASS/FAIL exit codes (already exit-code-authoritative, verifier.py) not to model prose, and refusing to emit an envelope for an UNVERIFIED Python-write-with-no-sibling-test.
dependencies: The existing verifier (_auto_verify force-run sibling pytest) as the evidence source
audit_logger append for the acceptance entry
A small shared handoff-envelope module factored out of agent_coord.tree_snapshot

recommendations.title: Cryptographic agent identity: turn the honor-system control plane into a signed-attestation one
recommendations.what: Today the single defining weakness of the whole multi-agent surface is that identity is cooperative: agent_coord.py's docstring is candid it 'cannot wake an agent or replace human approval' and an agent that ignores the lease is 'not blocked at the filesystem level' — a --builder override is even silently stored as preferred_builder and skews future routing. Add per-agent keypairs (operator-issued, local, offline) so every lease claim, heartbeat, handoff, and verdict carries an Ed25519 signature over the canonical action + the current tree_snapshot. The control plane verifies the signature before mutating state; an unsigned or wrong-key mutation is refused fail-closed (same posture as the gateway). This does NOT make it enforcement-at-the-filesystem (still cooperative on the WRITE side), but it makes the LEDGER unforgeable: you can no longer impersonate the other agent's verdict, and the 'builder cannot approve their own handoff' rule becomes cryptographic, not string-equality on a self-reported name.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Multi-agent 'identity' in the field today is a string in a message ('I am the reviewer'). Binding agent identity to a keypair AND to the content-addressed tree state — so a verdict is signed proof 'agent K reviewed exactly tree T' — is a notarized multi-agent collaboration record that survives an adversarial or buggy peer. Combined with the existing fail-closed-on-drift, it's the first solo-built agent coordination plane where the audit trail is cryptographically attributable, not just hash-chained.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: agent_coord.py:289-309 (_claim_reviewer string-compares assigned_reviewer), :535-536 (record_verdict forbids self-approval by sender==reviewer string check) — the trust decisions exist but rest on honor-system names; HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE.md P-couplings ('agent_coord.py is a cooperative lock, not enforcement'; '--builder override skews future auto-routing'). The audit chain's redact-before-hash + genesis discipline (audit_logger.py:115-270) is the proven pattern to extend with signatures.
recommendations.risk: Local keypairs on the same single laptop that runs everything add ceremony without raising the threat ceiling much (an attacker with full disk write access can read the private keys too — the same root limit as 'the audit chain has no external anchoring'). It is genuinely valuable only once (a) a second physical machine / a real remote agent joins, or (b) paired with the off-box notarization below. Honest framing: this is identity HYGIENE that becomes a security boundary only with an out-of-band root of trust. Don't oversell it as enforcement on a single box.
dependencies: Off-box anchoring (recommendation 5) to make signatures meaningful against a full-write-access attacker
Operator key issuance flow
Canonical-action serialization for stable signing

recommendations.title: Earned-autonomy as a verifiable inter-colony SKILL/AUTONOMY MARKET
recommendations.what: The earned-autonomy ledger keys grants by the secret-redacted SHAPE of an action (autonomy.py:_normalize → signature), graduates on >=5 consecutive verified successes, revokes on one verified failure, and audits each auto-grant as its own chain entry. Today that evidence is private to one install. The frontier move: make an earned signature a PORTABLE, verifiable credential — a colony that has earned autonomy for 'create *.py in training_ground' can export a signed evidence bundle (the streak, the verifier verdicts that earned it, the audit-chain segment) that ANOTHER colony / a future swarm can IMPORT only after re-verifying the evidence locally — it cannot trust the exporter's claim of the streak, it must re-derive trust by re-running the verifier on the same shape, or accept it strictly on probation (streak resets to local 0, never to the imported count). Pheromone trails (skills.py SUPERSEDES-not-DELETE, DIRECT-evidence-only promotion) get the same treatment: a trail is a tradeable artifact that ranks but can never launder a candidate to 'verified' without local DIRECT evidence — exactly the existing correctness boundary, now across colonies.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Agent 'markets' and 'skill libraries' in the literature trade PROMPTS or weights or trust SCORES on reputation. A market where the unit of exchange is verifier-anchored evidence, and the buyer is structurally forbidden from trusting the seller's score (must re-earn or accept only as probation), is a trustless skill market — the same 'co-occurrence can never launder into verified' invariant that makes the local skill trails sound, generalized to a population of colonies. No one is trading earned-autonomy-as-revocable-credential keyed to a redacted action shape; that primitive only exists here.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: autonomy.py:82-115 (_normalize + signature: scope-bound, secret-redacted shape) + :151-221 (record_outcome: streak math, instant revoke, returns evidence for audit) + tool_agent.py:711-745 (the auto-grant re-dispatches through the SAME gated path and the forced verify records back, revoking on failure); skills.py asymmetric pheromone 0.70805/1.04252 with DIRECT-evidence-only promotion (>=3 verified AND >=0.8). The 'same pheromone math as skill learning, applied to autonomy' is already the system's stated differentiator.
recommendations.risk: Two hard risks. (1) Invariant risk: an imported earned signature that auto-grants on the BUYER's box without local re-verification would be a catastrophic violation of evidence-over-model AND could widen the GREEN surface a human never authorized — the design MUST force import-to-probation (local streak=0) and RED-un-earnable still holds (autonomy.py refuses RED, execute_approved re-refuses). (2) It is genuinely premature: there is ONE install today; a 'market' needs >1 colony and a better brain to produce diverse earned shapes. Honest call: design the portable evidence-bundle format now, ship the cross-colony trading only once a real second colony exists.
dependencies: VA2A handoff envelope (rec 1) as the transport for evidence bundles
Cryptographic identity (rec 2) so a bundle's origin is attributable
A better brain (the model ceiling) to produce a non-trivial diversity of earned shapes worth trading

recommendations.title: Parallel heterogeneous-model swarm with per-leg evidence quorum (break the 'one weak brain, sequential' ceiling)
recommendations.what: swarm.py candidly runs workers SEQUENTIALLY by design ('one weak local model, bounded RAM; the swarm SHAPE is the contribution — a stronger/parallel runtime can dispatch the same legs concurrently'). The futuristic move the architecture is explicitly built to receive: dispatch the N independent worker legs CONCURRENTLY across HETEROGENEOUS models (e.g. a 14B local coder for write-legs, a fast 7B for read/triage legs, optionally a cloud model for the synthesizer) — each still over the SAME gated executor, same mechanical tool subset (enforced at tool_agent._dispatch:1042, not by prompt), same audit chain. Crucially, add an EVIDENCE QUORUM for high-stakes subtasks: spawn 2-3 heterogeneous workers on the same critical subtask and accept the result only where their VERIFIER verdicts agree (not where their prose agrees) — a deterministic, evidence-based ensemble vote, where a disagreement escalates to human exactly like a YELLOW pause. The stigmergic medium (sandbox + trails) already supports concurrent deposits with no shared mutable state, so the colony scales out without a message bus.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Model-ensemble voting today aggregates model OUTPUTS (majority text, judge-model). An ensemble that votes on independent VERIFIER verdicts — deterministic exit codes, not model opinions — over heterogeneous models running in one fail-closed cage, where a tie escalates to the human via the existing approval pause, is a verifier-quorum swarm. It fuses heterogeneous-model ensembles with evidence-over-model and supervised human-in-the-loop in a way no agent framework does, because none of them have a deterministic per-action verdict to vote on.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: swarm.py:180-197 (sequential-by-design, explicitly noting a parallel runtime can dispatch the same legs; workers share NO mutable state, stigmergy via sandbox + trail field) + tool_agent.py:645-648,1042 (caste tool subset enforced MECHANICALLY, so heterogeneous workers stay caged) + model_selector.select_model with require_tools (deterministic per-task model routing already exists). SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE.md confirms swarm is wired (main.py:1582) and tested end-to-end.
recommendations.risk: Real resource ceiling: this Windows laptop (16GB, GPU shared with the LLM and the superbrain's heavy cortex shader) cannot hold 3 concurrent 14B models — true parallelism is gated on the same RAM/model situation the whole assessment names as THE ceiling. Mitigate by making quorum opt-in for flagged high-stakes subtasks only, and by allowing the heterogeneous mix to include a cloud model leg (Bedrock is already wired, region-gated) so the local box isn't the only host. Also: shared MAX_REPLAYS/TURN_TIMEOUT caps (HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE footgun) will truncate a fan-out — must be per-worker, not per-turn.
dependencies: A better local brain or a cloud leg for the heavy workers (the model ceiling)
Per-worker replay/timeout budgets (current shared caps truncate swarm runs)
VA2A envelopes (rec 1) so concurrent worker handoffs are content-addressed, not racing on the shared conversation

recommendations.title: Off-box notarization of the audit head — the external root of trust that makes ALL multi-agent claims tamper-evident
recommendations.what: Every multi-agent guarantee above ultimately rests on the SHA-256 audit chain, and the chain's named ceiling is that it has no external anchoring: an attacker (or a buggy agent) with full write access can recompute the whole chain forward from a tampered entry into a self-consistent forgery (audit_logger.py; flagged in BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md:58). For a multi-agent world this is the load-bearing gap — a forged ledger means a forged verdict, a forged earned-autonomy streak, a forged handoff. The move: periodically notarize the audit head hash (and the coordination.db verdict head) off-box — append-only to a separate device/file the running process cannot rewrite, or a cheap public timestamping anchor — so any divergence between the live head and the last notarized head is detectable. Surface a divergence as the EXISTING superbrain TAMPER alarm (the UI already flips to TAMPER + tritone on /audit/verify valid:false). This is the minimum that turns 'agents collaborate under one audit chain' from a same-box honor system into a genuinely tamper-EVIDENT one.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: It is not novel as a primitive (notarization is old), but it is first-of-its-kind in THIS composition: the moment the head is externally anchored, the entire stack — verifier-quorum swarm, portable earned-autonomy credentials, signed agent identity, content-addressed handoffs — inherits external tamper-evidence for free, because every one of them already writes its decision as a distinct chain entry (earned-autonomy entry, verdict, handoff snapshot). One small anchor retroactively hardens five multi-agent guarantees. That leverage is the novel part.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: audit_logger.py:115-270 (O(n) verify_chain, genesis=64 zeros, broken_at + linkage-vs-tamper distinction) — the chain is already designed to detect divergence; it just lacks an external comparand. The frontend already RENDERS divergence honestly (SYSTEM_TRUE_PICTURE.md §3.3: /audit/verify valid:false → TAMPER shield + sustained tritone). PLAN.md watchlist explicitly names off-box notarization as 'the fix if/when this matters enough to attack' — multi-agent scale-out is exactly when it starts to matter.
recommendations.risk: On a single-operator local-first box this is correctly LOW priority today (the threat model is one trusted user) — the honest risk is doing it prematurely and adding ops burden for a threat that isn't live yet. It only earns its weight the moment a SECOND agent/machine that the operator doesn't fully control can write to the chain (i.e. once recs 1-4 create a real multi-party surface). Sequence it WITH the first true multi-party feature, not before. Must stay local-first: an external anchor that phones a cloud service by default would violate the privacy invariant — make it an offline append-only device/file first, public timestamping strictly opt-in.
dependencies: A second writer/agent existing (otherwise it's anchoring a single trusted user's own chain)
The existing /audit/verify + superbrain TAMPER surface as the divergence read-out

advices.northStar: Make the verifier-anchored handoff the universal law of agent collaboration: ship VA2A (rec 1) so that EVERY agent-to-agent edge in the system — swarm worker→synthesizer, caste coder→reviewer, and Claude→Codex — refuses to act on a peer's claim until it has re-derived that claim from the sandbox + the verifier ledger, with each acceptance a distinct audit-chain entry. That single move turns 'trust the evidence, not the model' from an intra-agent invariant into an INTER-agent protocol, and it is the one frontier piece this system is uniquely positioned to invent because it already built the primitive (hash-pinned, fail-closed-on-drift review) and proved it across a process boundary.
advices.honestCaveat: The multi-agent surface here is architecturally first-of-its-kind but operationally still SINGLE-PARTY and SEQUENTIAL, and I won't pretend otherwise. The swarm runs one weak local model's workers one after another (swarm.py says so plainly); the castes are 'architecture proven / 7B-limited' and need a 14B+ brain to produce real division-of-labor rather than the same model wearing three hats; agent_coord.py is a cooperative disk lock whose identity is honor-system, and on a single laptop it is NOT an enforcement boundary. The genuinely novel, verified, under-test parts are the PRIMITIVES — the hash-pinned fail-closed-on-drift handoff, the RED-un-earnable evidence-keyed autonomy ledger, the DIRECT-evidence-only pheromone promotion, the mechanically-gated caste tool subsets. Everything I recommend is generalizing those proven primitives to N minds; none of it becomes a real multi-AGENT system (rather than one mind playing many roles) until the model ceiling lifts and a genuine second party exists. Most of the security value (signed identity, off-box anchoring) is also latent on one box — real only when there's an untrusted peer to defend against. Build the protocols now because they're sound and the seeds are real; don't claim the colony is at scale until the brain and the second party are.

advices.persona: Category-creation & "first-of-its-kind" positioning strategist — honest, technical, no hype. I name the category a system legitimately creates, find the defensible moat (usually the discipline nobody finishes), pick the sharpest wedge, and design the demonstrations that convert a skeptic. I refuse claims I cannot ground in shipped, tested source.
advices.horizonThesis: This system has earned the right to NAME a category — and naming it is itself the highest-leverage move left, because the engineering moat (an evidence-locked, fail-closed, hash-chain-audited, structurally-un-self-approving core wired to a living-mind face that cannot fake activity) is already built and is precisely the part almost no team finishes. The category is the Evidence-Locked Supervised Personal AI-OS: a weak local model proposes, deterministic/tested/audited machinery decides, the operator's authority is a widening-but-never-lowerable floor, and the interface is a faithful read-out of that cage rather than a dashboard bolted on. The frontier here is not more capability — it is making the claim LEGIBLE and PROVABLE: turning the invariant ("trust the evidence, not the model") from an internal property into an externally verifiable artifact a skeptic can re-run, re-hash, and watch refuse. The moat deepens not by adding features but by making the discipline itself demonstrable, reproducible, and externally anchored.

recommendations.title: The Refusal Reel: a one-command, self-running adversarial gauntlet that PROVES the cage to a skeptic in 90 seconds
recommendations.what: A single driver (`prove.py`, modeled on the existing fail-closed allowlist drivers) that runs a fixed adversarial script against the LIVE HTTP surface and emits a signed transcript + the superbrain rendering it live: (1) submit a RED command, watch the gateway refuse it; (2) approve that RED command via the real one-click endpoint, watch `execute_approved` STILL refuse it ('Human approval cannot authorise a RED action'); (3) earn autonomy on a YELLOW shape through N verified successes, watch it auto-execute as `earned_autonomy`, then inject ONE verified failure and watch the streak reset and the next identical action drop back to a human pause; (4) tamper one byte in `aios_audit.db` and watch `/api/v1/audit/verify` flip to TAMPER with the exact `broken_at` index and the superbrain shield go to the sustained tritone alarm. The output is a timestamped, hash-chained 'refusal reel' (JSONL evidence + the 5 screen states) anyone can re-run and diff against a golden.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Almost every 'safe agent' demo shows the system DOING things well. This is the inverse and far rarer artifact: a reproducible demonstration whose entire value is what the system REFUSES even when you (the human) tell it not to — including refusing your own approval at the RED floor. A skeptic cannot dismiss it as cherry-picked because they re-run the exact script against the same fail-closed code; the refusals are structural, not prompt-tuned. No agent product ships a self-running 'here is the floor you cannot lower' proof.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: Built entirely on shipped, tested seams: `executor.py:436-443` RED-un-grantable-even-after-approval (test_execute_approved_still_refuses_red); `autonomy.py` one-failure-resets-streak; `audit_logger.py:115-270` O(n) verify with broken_at + linkage-vs-tamper; the existing fail-closed evidence drivers (`curriculum_evidence_driver.py`, `swarm_demo.py`, `earn_demo.py`) that already stream `/api/generate` SSE through approvals with an `^...$`-anchored allowlist; and the superbrain's tested TAMPER/RED reactions (soundEngine.test.ts: VERIFICATION RED never plays the success tick; aiosAdapter.test.ts honest LINK OFFLINE).
recommendations.risk: The tamper step writes to a copy of the audit DB, never the live ledger, or it becomes a footgun. The driver must reuse the single-sourced allowlist helpers (not re-implement them) or it weakens the very guarantee it demonstrates. Honest scoping: it proves the CONTRACTS (gating/refusal/verify) are correct — it does not prove real shell-quoting or container-flag behavior, which the test suite also cannot, so the reel must say so.
dependencies: aios/core/executor.py
aios/core/autonomy.py
aios/security/audit_logger.py
curriculum_evidence_driver.py
aios/api/main.py (/api/generate SSE + /api/v1/audit/verify)

recommendations.title: External anchoring: turn the local hash chain into an externally-verifiable root of trust (the one move that makes the audit claim un-dismissable)
recommendations.what: Add a periodic, off-box notarization of the audit chain HEAD hash: a small `anchor` action that takes the current head `current_hash` and writes it to an append-only out-of-band sink (signed file on a separate device, a git commit to a second remote, or a timestamping authority), then records the anchor as its own audit entry. Pair it with a `verify --since-anchor` mode that re-walks the chain from the last externally-witnessed head, so a skeptic can confirm not just internal linkage but that the head existed at time T and has not been silently recomputed-forward since. Keep it OFF by default and operator-triggered, consistent with local-first/single-operator.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: The honest ceiling everyone cites for a single-laptop hash chain is 'an attacker with full write access can recompute a self-consistent forgery.' Almost no personal/local AI system closes this at all. A supervised personal AI-OS whose self-modification ledger is periodically anchored to an out-of-band witness — so even total local compromise cannot rewrite history without detection at the next verify — converts 'tamper-evident on this box' into 'tamper-evident, period.' That is the difference between a demo-grade audit log and a forensically defensible one, and it is genuinely novel for a solo, local-first build.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The chain already computes the right primitive: `compute_entry_hash(previous_hash, timestamp, actor, payload, zone)` with redact-before-hash (`audit_logger.py:115-178`), genesis=64 zeros, and O(n) verify returning broken_at. The fix is named explicitly as the right one in the system's own docs (HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE 'audit chain has no external anchoring … periodic off-box notarization of the head hash is the fix'; PLAN watchlist). This builds on the strongest seam (the redact-before-hash chain) rather than touching the frozen security core's classification logic.
recommendations.risk: Anchoring touches the audit subsystem — sacrosanct, so it must be ADDITIVE (a new actor/entry + a read-only verify mode), never altering compute_entry_hash or the append path; needs explicit go before touching frozen-core-adjacent code. The out-of-band sink introduces an availability/privacy consideration (where the head hash lives) that must respect local-first: a head hash leaks nothing about payloads (redact-before-hash already strips secrets), but the operator must choose the witness. Over-claiming risk: anchoring proves no-silent-rewrite-since-last-anchor, not real-time integrity between anchors — say that plainly.
dependencies: aios/security/audit_logger.py
aios/api/main.py (/api/v1/audit/verify)
operator decision on the out-of-band witness (second device / second git remote / TSA)

recommendations.title: The Earned-Autonomy Ledger as a public, replayable 'trust receipt' — the category's signature artifact
recommendations.what: Promote the earned-autonomy evidence from an internal table to a first-class, exportable, human-legible artifact: a 'trust receipt' for every auto-grant that states, in one screen, the action SHAPE (type + scope-bound, secret-redacted target pattern), the exact streak of VERIFIED successes that earned it, the audit-chain entry id under actor `earned-autonomy`, and a one-click REVOKE. Render it as a dedicated, micro-detailed read-out in the superbrain HUD (a real-data panel, never a redesign) that shows the GREEN surface widening and — on a verified failure — visibly narrowing in real time. Add a `--why-autonomous <shape>` query that prints the full evidence trail behind any granted shape.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Autonomy in agent systems is almost always a trust SCORE or a config flag — opaque, model-derived, and irreversible-by-narration. Here it is a mechanically-earned, evidence-keyed, instantly-revocable, RED-un-earnable capability whose entire justification is auditable and replayable. Exposing that as a per-grant 'trust receipt' makes the system the first to let an operator (or skeptic) ask 'WHY is this autonomous?' and get a deterministic, evidence-backed, falsifiable answer — and watch the surface contract the instant the evidence does. That legibility IS the category.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The full mechanism is shipped and tested: `autonomy.py` AutonomyLedger keys by secret-redacted action shape, requires MIN_SUCCESSES verified, resets on one verified failure, and writes a distinct audit-chain entry under actor 'earned-autonomy'; the SSE `earned_autonomy` frame already exists (`main.py:1618-1623`) and the superbrain already renders it as an 'AUTONOMY ⚡N' pill / 'AUTONOMOUS ACTION'. This is surfacing + querying existing evidence, not new control logic — and it respects FIDELITY-IS-SACRED (additive real-data panel, micro-detailing, no redesign).
recommendations.risk: The receipt must render the REDACTED shape and streak only — never raw values or secrets (the ledger already stores stripped/redacted shapes, so the export path must not re-introduce raw payloads). UI work is bound by FIDELITY-IS-SACRED: canon tag + goldens + before/after screenshots before any visual change, and it must read honestly dormant when earned-autonomy is OFF (the default) rather than implying capability that isn't enabled. Risk of over-selling: earned-autonomy is OFF by default and the GREEN surface is tiny by design — the receipt panel must make that limitation legible, not paper over it.
dependencies: aios/core/autonomy.py
aios/api/main.py (earned_autonomy SSE frame + /development/autonomy)
frontend/src/superbrain/lib/aiosAdapter.ts + SuperbrainHUD.tsx
canon tag + goldens (FIDELITY-IS-SACRED)

recommendations.title: The Cage Conformance Spec: write down the invariant as a portable, testable standard others could adopt — claim the category by defining it
recommendations.what: Author a short, precise CONFORMANCE document — 'The Evidence-Locked Supervised Agent: 7 invariants and their conformance tests' — that states each load-bearing invariant as a falsifiable property and CITES the exact test that proves it in this repo: (I1) fail-closed-everywhere → RED on empty/unknown/exception (test_fail_closed_on_internal_exception); (I2) human approval cannot authorize RED (test_execute_approved_still_refuses_red); (I3) redact-before-hash so no-secret-persistence and chain-validity coexist (test_secret_is_redacted_before_persistence); (I4) cross-process single valid chain (test_concurrent_appends_keep_one_valid_chain); (I5) promotion only on DIRECT verifier evidence, never co-occurrence (skills.py promotion gates); (I6) autonomy earned by verified evidence, revoked by one verified failure, RED-un-earnable; (I7) the interface cannot fake activity (soundEngine/aiosAdapter dormancy tests). Ship it as a versioned spec a third party could run their own system against.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: Categories are won by whoever writes the rulebook. 'Responsible/safe AI' is a vibe; 'evidence-over-model carried through 7 named, individually-test-pinned invariants with a portable conformance suite' is a STANDARD. No personal/local AI-OS has published a falsifiable conformance spec where every invariant is bound to a runnable test — that turns 'trust me, it's supervised' into 'here is the bar; here is the test; re-run it.' It also future-proofs the claim: as the system grows, conformance is the gate that keeps the category honest.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: Every invariant is ALREADY pinned by a real, named test in a 512-passing, 90%-coverage suite with the 'real gateway, fake runner' discipline (fakes only the non-deterministic edges) — the calibre brief and BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE enumerate exactly these tests. This is naming and packaging existing, verified contracts as a spec — the lowest-risk, highest-positioning-leverage move available, and it directly serves the 'report at ~90% honesty' standard because the spec must also list where the system is NOT yet ahead.
recommendations.risk: Pure positioning power only if it stays honest: the spec MUST include the negative section (the 1 skipped symlink test never runs on the operator's Windows box; no test exercises a real subprocess/Docker/Ollama; the 3D scene has zero unit tests; autonomous surface is tiny by design; isolation is opt-in). A conformance doc that hides its own gaps would violate the operator's ~90%-honesty standard and be trivially debunked. It is a document, not a moat by itself — its value is realized only alongside the Refusal Reel (Rec 1) that makes it executable.
dependencies: the existing test suite (tests/, frontend vitest, lab vitest)
calibre evidenceAnchors
Rec 1 (Refusal Reel) as the executable companion

recommendations.title: Semantic recall for skill/curriculum trails — let the evidence-locked learning layer recall by MEANING, the one upgrade that makes the proven mechanism visibly smarter without touching the floor
recommendations.what: Close the two named lexical seams: route skill/lesson/curriculum recall through the FAISS semantic stack that already exists for semantic_memory (currently these recalls are pure-lexical / exact-prompt-string), and let BM25 run over a union of the FAISS candidate pool AND a direct lexical pass so a pure-lexical match FAISS never surfaces isn't missed. Critically, keep the PROMOTION gate untouched: semantic similarity may improve RECALL/RANKING only — it can NEVER launder a candidate into 'verified.' Direct verifier evidence remains the only path to trusted, exactly as today.
recommendations.whyFirstOfItsKind: This is the rare upgrade that increases apparent intelligence while STRENGTHENING the evidence-over-model invariant rather than eroding it: the model/embedder gets more say over what is RECALLED, but zero say over what is TRUSTED. A learning system where semantic similarity widens recall but is structurally forbidden from promoting to verified is a clean, novel separation of 'what might be relevant' (soft, model-driven) from 'what is proven' (hard, verifier-driven) — the same discipline as the rest of the cage, applied to memory retrieval.
recommendations.groundsInCalibre: The seams are explicitly documented as the obvious next retrieval lever (HIDDEN_KNOWLEDGE 'two named lexical recall seams'; PLAN S1 semantic-recall layer ~3-4 days). The FAISS stack, IndexIDMap crash-consistency, and hybrid 0.25·BM25+0.45·FAISS+0.30·decay retrieval already exist for semantic_memory; the promotion invariant (≥3 verified successes AND ≥0.8 rate, DIRECT evidence only, supersede-not-delete) is in `skills.py` and pinned by test. This extends a proven retrieval path to two stores that don't yet use it.
recommendations.risk: The hard line is that semantic recall must remain advisory-to-RANKING-only; a careless wiring that let an embedder-surfaced candidate satisfy a promotion gate would violate the core invariant and is a non-starter — needs a test asserting semantic recall can never promote. Cold-start embedder load is on the request thread (a known perf hotspot), so widening embedder use has a first-call latency cost. Honest caveat: on a 7-8B/limited-RAM box the bigger intelligence ceiling is the model itself; this helps recall, it does not make the brain reason better.
dependencies: aios/memory/skills.py
aios/memory/curriculum.py
aios/memory/semantic.py + embeddings.py (FAISS/IndexIDMap)
aios/memory/retrieval.py (hybrid fusion)
a new test pinning 'semantic recall never promotes to verified'

advices.northStar: Ship the Refusal Reel (Rec 1) and the Cage Conformance Spec (Rec 4) together as one artifact: a single command a skeptic runs that re-proves all seven invariants live — including the system refusing the operator's own RED approval and the GREEN surface narrowing the instant a verified failure lands — and emits a hash-chained, re-diffable transcript. That pairing is what makes the category 'Evidence-Locked Supervised Personal AI-OS' legibly first-of-its-kind: not a pitch, but a falsifiable, re-runnable proof that the floor exists and the human cannot lower it. Everything else (external anchoring, trust receipts, semantic recall) deepens the same moat — the discipline most teams never finish, now made executable on demand.
advices.honestCaveat: Where this is NOT yet ahead, told straight: the category claim is about the CONTROL ARCHITECTURE and its DISCIPLINE, not about intelligence or autonomy — and a skeptic will rightly press both. (1) The brain is the ceiling: 7-8B local, planning is advisory, calibration is a no-op on a cold DB, castes are 'architecture proven / 7B-limited,' curriculum matching is exact-prompt-string. This is a supervised assistant that can safely act, not a system that genuinely runs itself. (2) The autonomous surface is near-zero by design (host GREEN = echo/pwd) and earned-autonomy is OFF by default — so 'self-improving' is true and shipped but deliberately narrow; do not let the trust-receipt panel imply a wide autonomous surface that isn't enabled. (3) The strongest isolation (hardened Docker) is opt-in; the default host backend is candidly 'not an OS isolation boundary,' so the cage is a POLICY/audit cage by default, a true OS cage only with Docker present. (4) The 3D superbrain — the 'face that cannot lie' — has ZERO unit tests; its honesty rests on golden PNGs and puppeteer probes a human eyeballs, so a hold-choreography or wave-anchoring regression would not fail CI. (5) The tests prove the CONTRACTS (gating, refusal, verify, chain integrity), not real shell-quoting, real container flags, or real model behavior. The category is legitimately first-of-its-kind on the merged axis — evidence-locked cage + earned/revocable/RED-un-earnable autonomy + a real-data living-mind face — but each axis alone has prior art, and the moat is the FINISHED DISCIPLINE, which is exactly why making that discipline executable and externally anchored (Recs 1, 2, 4) matters more right now than any new capability.
